# Topic Dossier: ux

Accessible conversation excerpts tagged by keyword. Source dump, not polished synthesis.


## 1. 2026-07-03T16:50:51.040Z - autoquant_origin - user

That’s a great name for the Codex sandbox.

I’d use Analytics Shiny App as the place to build the product shell around AutoPlots without risking AutoPlots itself.

The project mission should be:

Analytics Shiny App is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

It lets users:
- load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange plots into grids/tabs/sections
- preview outputs
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

The first AGENTS.md / Codex instruction file should be strict:

# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)

Generated layout code must use:

AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

For the first Codex task, I’d keep it extremely bounded:

Create the initial Shiny app skeleton.

Requirements:
- app.R at project root
- app loads AutoPlots
- app has tabs: Data, Plots, Layout, Export
- Data tab can upload one CSV and preview it
- Plots tab lets user choose PlotType from Area, Line, Bar, Scatter, Histogram
- User can choose XVar, YVar, GroupVar from uploaded data columns
- User can enter title.text
- App renders one preview plot
- App shows generated AutoPlots R code
- Do not implement multi-plot layout yet
- Do not implement Electron packaging yet

Then second task:

Add multi-plot support.

Requirements:
- User can add current preview plot to a plot list
- Show list of created plots with names p1, p2, p3
- Layout tab previews AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Generated code includes plot creation code and display_plots_sections() code

That progression keeps it safe and gets you to the real product quickly.

# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

```r
AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)
```

Generated layout code must use:

```r
AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
```

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

# Analytics Shiny App Instructions

This repo is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

The app should let users:
- upload or load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange charts into grids, tabs, and sections
- preview the result
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

AutoPlots API rules:
- Use high-level AutoPlots functions such as AutoPlots::Area(), AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Scatter(), AutoPlots::Histogram(), AutoPlots::Density().
- Do not use echarts4r verbs directly unless explicitly instructed.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly as substitutes.
- Do not introduce nested option objects.
- Generated code must use flat AutoPlots arguments.
- Data columns are passed as strings.
- Common arguments include Theme, title.text, title.subtext, Height, Width, ShowLabels, MouseScroll, legend.show, xAxis.title, yAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate.

Composition API:
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Prefer display_plots_sections() as the default report layout.

Export API:
- Use AutoPlots::save_widget() for self-contained HTML.
- Use AutoPlots::save_image() for PNG.
- Future output should include generated R code.

Reference:
- Use ../AutoPlots/README.md or docs/AutoPlots_README.md as read-only AutoPlots reference.
- Do not modify AutoPlots source files from this repo.

library(shiny)
library(AutoPlots)

plot_registry <- list(
  Area = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Line = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Bar = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Scatter = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Histogram = list(mappings = c("XVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Density = list(mappings = c("XVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Pie = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Donut = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  HeatMap = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar", "ZVar"), optional_mappings = character()),
  CorrMatrix = list(mappings = c("CorrVars"), optional_mappings = character())
)

plot_types <- names(plot_registry)

theme_choices <- c(
  "dark", "auritus", "azul", "bee-inspired", "blue", "caravan", "carp",
  "chalk", "cool", "dark-blue", "dark-bold", "dark-digerati",
  "dark-fresh-cut", "dark-mushroom", "eduardo", "essos", "forest",
  "fresh-cut", "fruit", "gray", "green", "halloween", "helianthus",
  "infographic", "inspired", "jazz", "london", "macarons", "macarons2",
  "mint", "purple-passion", "red", "red-velvet", "roma", "royal",
  "sakura", "v5", "walden", "wef", "weforum"
)

empty_choice <- function(label = "(none)") {
  stats::setNames("", label)
}

column_choices <- function(data, include_none = FALSE, label = "(none)") {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(empty_choice(label))
  }

  choices <- names(data)
  if (include_none) {
    return(c(empty_choice(label), choices))
  }

  choices
}

r_string <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return("NULL")
  }

  deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)
}

selected_value <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

selected_vector <- function(value) {
  value <- selected_value(value)
  if (is.null(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

logical_value <- function(value) {
  isTRUE(value)
}

numeric_or_null <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "") || is.na(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  as.numeric(value)
}

plot_spec <- function(plot_type) {
  plot_registry[[plot_type]]
}

mapping_input_id <- function(mapping) {
  paste0("mapping_", mapping)
}

mapping_value <- func ... [truncated]

library(shiny)
library(AutoPlots)

plot_registry <- list(
  Area = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Line = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Bar = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Scatter = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Histogram = list(mappings = c("XVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Density = list(mappings = c("XVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Pie = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Donut = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  HeatMap = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar", "ZVar"), optional_mappings = character()),
  CorrMatrix = list(mappings = c("CorrVars"), optional_mappings = character())
)

plot_types <- names(plot_registry)

theme_choices <- c(
  "dark", "auritus", "azul", "bee-inspired", "blue", "caravan", "carp",
  "chalk", "cool", "dark-blue", "dark-bold", "dark-digerati",
  "dark-fresh-cut", "dark-mushroom", "eduardo", "essos", "forest",
  "fresh-cut", "fruit", "gray", "green", "halloween", "helianthus",
  "infographic", "inspired", "jazz", "london", "macarons", "macarons2",
  "mint", "purple-passion", "red", "red-velvet", "roma", "royal",
  "sakura", "v5", "walden", "wef", "weforum"
)

empty_choice <- function(label = "(none)") {
  stats::setNames("", label)
}

column_choices <- function(data, include_none = FALSE, label = "(none)") {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(empty_choice(label))
  }

  choices <- names(data)
  if (include_none) {
    return(c(empty_choice(label), choices))
  }

  choices
}

r_string <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return("NULL")
  }

  deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)
}

selected_value <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

selected_vector <- function(value) {
  value <- selected_value(value)
  if (is.null(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

logical_value <- function(value) {
  isTRUE(value)
}

numeric_or_null <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "") || is.na(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  as.numeric(value)
}

plot_spec <- function(plot_type) {
  plot_registry[[plot_type]]
}

mapping_input_id <- function(mapping) {
  paste0("mapping_", mapping)
}

mapping_value <- func ... [truncated]

Output:
# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

```r
AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)
```

Generated layout code must use:

```r
AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
```

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

# Analytics Shiny App Instructions

This repo is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

The app should let users:
- upload or load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange charts into grids, tabs, and sections
- preview the result
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

AutoPlots API rules:
- Use high-level AutoPlots functions such as AutoPlots::Area(), AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Scatter(), AutoPlots::Histogram(), AutoPlots::Density().
- Do not use echarts4r verbs directly unless explicitly instructed.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly as substitutes.
- Do not introduce nested option objects.
- Generated code must use flat AutoPlots arguments.
- Data columns are passed as strings.
- Common arguments include Theme, title.text, title.subtext, Height, Width, ShowLabels, MouseScroll, legend.show, xAxis.title, yAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate.

Composition API:
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Prefer display_plots_sections() as the default report layout.

Export API:
- Use AutoPlots::save_widget() for self-contained HTML.
- Use AutoPlots::save_image() for PNG.
- Future output should include generated R code.

Reference:
- Use ../AutoPlots/README.md or docs/AutoPlots_README.md as read-only AutoPlots reference.
- Do not modify AutoPlots source files from this repo.

#' @param EchartsLabels character
#' @param TimeLine logical
#' @param MouseScroll logical, zoom via mouse scroll
#' @param AggMethod character
#' @param GroupVar Character variable
#' @param Debug Debugging purposes
#' @return plot
#' @export
PartialDependence.Box <- function(dt = NULL,
                                  PreAgg = TRUE,
                                  SampleSize = 100000L,
                                  XVar = NULL,
                                  YVar = NULL,
                                  ZVar = NULL,
                                  GroupVar = NULL,
                                  YVarTrans = "Identity",
                                  XVarTrans = "Identity",
                                  ZVarTrans = "Identity",
                                  FacetRows = 1,
                                  FacetCols = 1,
                                  FacetLevels = NULL,
                                  NumberBins = 20,
                                  AggMethod = "mean",
                                  Height = NULL,
                                  Width = NULL,
                                  ShowLabels = FALSE,
                                  Theme = "dark",
                                  MouseScroll = FALSE,
                                  EchartsLabels = FALSE,
                                  TimeLine = FALSE,
                                  title.text = NULL,
                                  yAxis.title = NULL,
                                  xAxis.title = NULL,
                                  Debug = FALSE) {

  if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
  })

  GroupVar <- NULL

  # Minimize data before moving on
  if(Debug) print("Plot.PartialDependence.Box # Minimize data before moving on")
  Ncols <- ncol(dt)
  if(Ncols > 3L) {
    dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt[, .SD, .SDcols = c(YVar, XVar, ZVar)])
  } else {
    dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt)
  }

  # If actual is in factor form, convert to numeric
  if(Debug) print("Plot.PartialDependence.Box # If actual is in factor form, convert to numeric")
  if(!is.numeric(dt1[[YVar]])) {
    data.table::set(dt1, j = YVar, value = as.numeric(as.character(dt1[[YVar]])))
  }

  # Add a column that ranks predicted values
  dt1[, eval(XVar) := as.character(round(data.table::frank(get(XVar)) * (NumberBins) / .N) / NumberBi ... [truncated]

#' @param FacetCols Defaults to 1 which causes no faceting to occur horizontally. Otherwise, supply a numeric value for the number of output grid columns
#' @param FacetLevels Faceting rows x columns is the max number of levels allowed in a grid. If your GroupVar has more you can supply the levels to display.

#' @param Width "200px"

#' @param ShowLabels character

                Debug = FALSE) {

  apply_theme_defaults(Theme, plot_type = "Pie", grouped = FALSE, env = environment())
  if(length(YVar) > 0L) YVar <- YVar[1L]
  if(length(XVar) > 0L) XVar <- XVar[1L]

  # Used multiple times
  check1 <- length(XVar) != 0 && length(YVar) != 0

  if(!PreAgg) {
    if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
      dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
    })
    aggFunc <- SummaryFunction(AggMethod)
  }

  # Convert factor to character
  if(length(GroupVar) > 0L && class(dt[[GroupVar]])[1L] == "factor") {
    dt[, eval(GroupVar) := as.character(get(GroupVar))]
  }

  if(length(XVar) > 0L && class(dt[[XVar]])[1L] == "factor") {
    dt[, eval(XVar) := as.character(get(XVar))]
  }

  # Create base plot object
  numvars <- c()
  byvars <- c()
  if(check1) {
    if(!PreAgg) {
      if(tryCatch({class(dt[[eval(YVar)]])[1L]}, error = function(x) "bla") %in% c('numeric','integer')) {
        numvars <- unique(c(numvars, YVar))
      } else {
        byvars <- unique(c(byvars, YVar))
      }
      if(tryCatch({class(dt[[eval(XVar)]])[1L]}, error = function(x) "bla") %in% c('numeric','integer')) {
        if(length(numvars) > 0) {
          x <- length(unique(dt[[XVar]]))
          y <- length(unique(dt[[YVar]]))
          if(x > y) {
            byvars <- unique(c(byvars, YVar))
            numvars[1L] <- XVar
          } else {
            byvars <- unique(c(byvars, XVar))
          }
        } else {
          numvars <- unique(c(numvars, XVar))
        }
      } else {
        byvars <- unique(c(byvars, XVar))
      }
      if(!is.null(byvars)) {
        temp <- dt[, lapply(.SD, noquote(aggFunc)), .SDcols = c(numvars), keyby = c(byvars)]
        for(i in byvars) {
          if(class(temp[[i]])[1L] %in% c('numeric','integer')) {
            temp[, eval(i) := as.character(get(i))]
          }
        }
      } else {
        temp <- dt[, lapply(.SD, noquote(aggFunc)), .SDcols = c(numvars)]
      }
    } else {
      temp <- data.table::copy(dt)
      numvars <- ColNameFilter(data = temp, Types = 'numeric')[[1L]]
      byvars <- unlist(ColNameFilter(data = temp, Types = "character"))
    }

    # Transformation
    if(YVarTrans != "Identity") {
      temp <- AutoTransformationCreate(data = temp, ColumnNames = numvars, Methods = YVarTrans)$Data
    }

    p1 <- echarts4r::e_charts_(
      temp,
      x = XVar,
      dispose = TRUE, ... [truncated]

  byvars <- prep$byvars

    p1 <- echarts4r::e_charts_(
      temp,
      x = XVar,
      dispose = TRUE,
      darkMode = TRUE,
      emphasis = list(focus = "series"),
      width = Width, height = Height)

    p1 <- echarts4r::e_pie_(
      e = p1,
      YVar,
      stack = XVar
    )

    if (ShowLabels) {
      p1 <- echarts4r::e_labels(
        e = p1,
        show = TRUE,
        formatter = htmlwidgets::JS(
          "function(params) {
             return params.name + ': ' + params.value.toFixed(1) + ' (' + params.percent.toFixed(1) + '%)';
           }"
        ),
        position = "outside"
      )
    }

    p1 <- echarts4r::e_theme(e = p1, name = Theme)

    p1 <- e_tooltip_full(
      e = p1,
      tooltip.show = tooltip.show,
      tooltip.trigger = tooltip.trigger,
      tooltip.backgroundColor = tooltip.backgroundColor,
      tooltip.borderColor = tooltip.borderColor,
      tooltip.borderWidth = tooltip.borderWidth,

  apply_theme_defaults(Theme, plot_type = "HeatMap", grouped = FALSE, env = environment())

  if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
  })

  # Convert factor to character
  if(length(ZVar) > 0L && class(dt[[ZVar]])[1L] %in% c("factor","character")) {
    dt[, eval(ZVar) := as.numeric(get(ZVar))]
  }
  if(length(XVar) > 0L && class(dt[[XVar]])[1L] == "factor") {
    dt[, eval(XVar) := as.character(get(XVar))]
  }
  if(length(YVar) > 0L && class(dt[[YVar]])[1L] == "factor") {
    dt[, eval(YVar) := as.character(get(YVar))]
  }

  # Subset cols
  dt1 <- dt[, .SD, .SDcols = c(XVar,YVar,ZVar)]
  x_check <- class(dt1[[XVar]])[1L] %in% c('numeric','integer')
  y_check <- class(dt1[[YVar]])[1L] %in% c('numeric','integer')
  x_y_num <- x_check && y_check
  x_num <- x_check && !y_check
  x_char <- !x_check && y_check
  all_char <- !x_check && !y_check

  # XVar == numeric or integer && YVar == numeric or integer

    # rank XVar and YVar

      dt1[, eval(XVar) := round(data.table::frank(dt1[[XVar]]) * NumberBins /.N) / NumberBins]
      dt1[, eval(YVar) := round(data.table::frank(dt1[[YVar]]) * NumberBins /.N) / NumberBins]

    # Transformation
    if(ZVarTrans != "Identity") {
      dt1 <- AutoTransformationCreate(data = dt1, ColumnNames = ZVar, Methods = ZVarTrans)$Data
    }

    # Formatting
    vals <- unique(scales::rescale(c(dt1[[ZVar]])))
    o <- order(vals, decreasing = FALSE)
    cols <- scales::col_numeric("Purples", domain = NULL)(vals)
    colz <- stats::setNames(data.frame(vals[o], cols[o]), NULL)
    data.table::setnames(dt1, ZVar, "Measure_Variable")
    data.table::setorderv(x = dt1, cols = c(XVar,YVar),c(1L,1L))

      cols <- scales::col_numeric("Purples", domain = NULL)(vals)

R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R

Problem:

  XCat = sample(LETTERS[1:8], 2000, TRUE),
  YNum = rnorm(2000, 50, 10),
  ZVal = runif(2000, 10, 500)
)

HeatMap(
  dt = dt,
  XVar = "XCat",
  YVar = "YNum",
  ZVar = "ZVal",
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "HeatMap Cat / Num"
)

The data appears in tooltip, but heatmap cells are not visible.

Likely cause:
For HeatMap, numeric X/Y axes are currently ranked/binned to decimal values like 0.04761904761904762. ECharts heatmap behaves more reliably when both heatmap axes are categorical coordinates. The prep helper should turn binned numeric axes into clean category labels for HeatMap.

Simplest acceptable fix:
- round binned numeric axis values to a reasonable number of digits, e.g. 4 or 6
- convert to character
- preserve ordering with setorderv before conversion where needed

2. Apply this for HeatMap cases:
- both_numeric
- x_categorical_y_numeric
- x_numeric_y_categorical

3. Ensure the prepared dt contains:
- XVar
- YVar
- value_var

4. Ensure HeatMap rendering uses value_var returned by prep.

- only touch vars that exist
- round numeric values

- keep ordered category-like labels

- only touch vars that exist

## 79. 2026-07-04T04:11:35.587Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

Implemented the 3D axis label polish in [R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R).

What changed:
- Added internal `.ap_apply_3d_axis_defaults()`.
- Applies `xAxis3D`, `yAxis3D`, and `zAxis3D` styling directly to the htmlwidget.
- Adds a JS formatter that rounds normal numeric labels to 4 digits, uses scientific notation for very small/large values, and truncates long category labels.
- Uses readable dark-theme defaults for axis titles, labels, axis lines, and split lines.
- Wired the helper into:
  - all four `BarPlot3D` branch returns
  - `Copula3D`
  - `Scatter3D`

Verified:
- `BarPlot3D` categorical/categorical, categorical/numeric, numeric/categorical, numeric/numeric all return `echarts4r/htmlwidget`.
- `Scatter3D` renders with numeric X/Y/Z and with optional `GroupVar`.
- `Copula3D` renders with and without `GroupVar`.
- Confirmed `xAxis3D/yAxis3D/zAxis3D` options now include the label formatter and dark label color.

Note: I left HeatMap untouched for this pass.

.ap_apply_3d_axis_defaults <- function(
  e,
  XVar,
  YVar,
  ZVar,
  xAxis.title = NULL,
  yAxis.title = NULL,
  zAxis.title = NULL,
  Theme = "dark"
) {
  dark_theme <- length(Theme) > 0L && grepl("dark", Theme[1L], ignore.case = TRUE)
  label_color <- if (dark_theme) "#E5E7EB" else "#374151"
  line_color <- if (dark_theme) "#6B7280" else "#9CA3AF"
  split_color <- if (dark_theme) "rgba(229,231,235,0.16)" else "rgba(55,65,81,0.16)"
  formatter <- htmlwidgets::JS(
    "function(value) {
       var text = String(value);
       var number = Number(value);
       if (text !== '' && isFinite(number)) {
         var abs = Math.abs(number);
         if (abs !== 0 && (abs < 0.001 || abs >= 10000)) {
           return number.toExponential(2);
         }
         return Number(number.toFixed(4)).toString();
       }
       return text.length > 18 ? text.slice(0, 17) + '...' : text;
     }"
  )

  axis_opts <- function(name) {
    list(
      name = name,
      nameGap = 24,
      nameTextStyle = list(
        color = label_color,
        fontFamily = "Segoe UI",
        fontSize = 13,
        fontWeight = "bold"
      ),
      axisLabel = list(
        color = label_color,
        fontFamily = "Segoe UI",
        fontSize = 11,
        formatter = formatter
      ),
      axisLine = list(lineStyle = list(color = line_color)),
      axisPointer = list(label = list(formatter = formatter)),
      splitLine = list(lineStyle = list(color = split_color))
    )
  }

  e$x$opts$xAxis3D <- axis_opts(if (length(xAxis.title) > 0L) xAxis.title else XVar)
  e$x$opts$yAxis3D <- axis_opts(if (length(yAxis.title) > 0L) yAxis.title else YVar)
  e$x$opts$zAxis3D <- axis_opts(if (length(zAxis.title) > 0L) zAxis.title else ZVar)
  e
}

.ap_top_grid_levels <- function(dt, category_var, value_var, n_levels) {
  if (!.ap_not_empty(category_var) || !category_var %in% names(dt)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Categorical axes are top-N filtered before grid aggregation.
  top_levels <- dt[
    ,
    lapply(.SD, mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    .SDcols = value_var,
    by = category_var
  ][order(-get(value_var))]

  top_levels[seq_len(min(n_levels, top_levels[, .N]))][[category_var]]
}

## 84. 2026-07-04T04:47:24.480Z - autoquant_origin - user

Alrighty, now that we've refactored the data aggregation stuff, we can move back to the app. Expose AutoPlots built-in aggregation controls in the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to use AutoPlots built-in PreAgg/AggMethod behavior from the plot builder.

Rules:
- Do not implement app-side data.table aggregation in this task.
- Do not modify AutoPlots in this task.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep registry-driven UI.
- Make small targeted changes.

Important AutoPlots semantics:
PreAgg = TRUE means the data is already plot-ready / already aggregated.
PreAgg = FALSE means AutoPlots should aggregate internally.

Therefore the UI should NOT expose a raw "PreAgg" checkbox.

Add user-facing controls:
- Auto-aggregate before plotting
- AggMethod

Implementation requirements:

1. Add option_registry entries for:
- AutoAggregate
- AggMethod

AutoAggregate:
- input_id = "auto_aggregate"
- label = "Auto-aggregate before plotting"
- type = "checkbox"
- default = FALSE

AggMethod:
- input_id = "agg_method"
- label = "AggMethod"
- type = "select"
- choices = c("mean", "sum", "median", "sd", "min", "max", "count")
- default = "mean"

2. Add AutoAggregate and AggMethod to plot_registry options only for plot types that currently support PreAgg/AggMethod in the app.

Add to:
- Line
- Area
- Step if present
- River if present
- Bar
- StackedBar if present
- Pie
- Donut
- Rosetype if present
- HeatMap
- BarPlot3D if present
- Radar if present
- Treemap if present

Do not add to:
- Histogram
- Density
- Scatter
- CorrMatrix
- Box
- WordCloud
- Parallel

3. Update option rendering:
- AggMethod should only be visible when AutoAggregate is TRUE if that is simple with the current dynamic UI.
- If conditional visibility is annoying, it is acceptable for AggMethod to always show for supported plot types, but generated args should still be correct.

4. Update option_value/build_plot_args.

Special handling:
- AutoAggregate should not become an argument named AutoAggregate.
- It should produce PreAgg.

If input$auto_aggregate is TRUE:
  args$PreAgg <- FALSE

If input$auto_aggregate is FALSE:
  args$PreAgg <- TRUE

AggMethod should produce:
  args$AggMethod <- input$agg_method

5. Generated code should show actual AutoPlots arguments.

Example:

Line(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "Date",
  YVar = "Spend",
  GroupVar = "Channel",
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "sum",
  Theme = "dark"
)

6. Build / Refresh Plot shou ... [truncated]

# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

```r
AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)
```

Generated layout code must use:

```r
AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
```

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

# Analytics Shiny App Instructions

This repo is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

The app should let users:
- upload or load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange charts into grids, tabs, and sections
- preview the result
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

AutoPlots API rules:
- Use high-level AutoPlots functions such as AutoPlots::Area(), AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Scatter(), AutoPlots::Histogram(), AutoPlots::Density().
- Do not use echarts4r verbs directly unless explicitly instructed.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly as substitutes.
- Do not introduce nested option objects.
- Generated code must use flat AutoPlots arguments.
- Data columns are passed as strings.
- Common arguments include Theme, title.text, title.subtext, Height, Width, ShowLabels, MouseScroll, legend.show, xAxis.title, yAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate.

Composition API:
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Prefer display_plots_sections() as the default report layout.

Export API:
- Use AutoPlots::save_widget() for self-contained HTML.
- Use AutoPlots::save_image() for PNG.
- Future output should include generated R code.

Reference:
- Use ../AutoPlots/README.md or docs/AutoPlots_README.md as read-only AutoPlots reference.
- Do not modify AutoPlots source files from this repo.

#' @param title_color CSS color for section titles.
#' @param subtitle_color CSS color for section subtitles.
#' @param font_family CSS font-family.
#' @param save_path Optional file path to save standalone HTML.
#' @param selfcontained Logical passed to htmlwidgets::saveWidget.
#' @param return_code Logical. If TRUE, return list(html = ..., code = ...).
#'
#' @return A browsable sectioned HTML report, or list(html, code) when return_code = TRUE.
#'
#' @export
display_plots_sections <- function(
    sections,
    cols = 2,
    collapsible = TRUE,
    open = TRUE,
    section_titles = NULL,
    section_subtitles = NULL,
    plot_titles = NULL,
    plot_subtitles = NULL,
    gap = "20px",
    section_gap = "22px",
    padding = "0px",
    card = TRUE,
    card_background = NULL,
    card_border = NULL,
    card_shadow = NULL,
    section_background = NULL,
    section_border = NULL,
    section_radius = "16px",
    section_padding = "18px",
    title_color = NULL,
    subtitle_color = NULL,
    font_family = "Segoe UI, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif",
    save_path = NULL,
    selfcontained = TRUE,
    return_code = FALSE
) {

  if (!is.list(sections) || length(sections) == 0L) {
    stop("`sections` must be a non-empty named list of plot lists.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  nsections <- length(sections)

  if (is.null(section_titles)) {
    nms <- names(sections)
    if (!is.null(nms) && all(nzchar(nms))) {
      section_titles <- nms
    } else {
      section_titles <- paste0("Section ", seq_len(nsections))
    }
  }

  section_titles <- .ap_normalize_labels(section_titles, nsections, "section_titles")
  section_subtitles <- .ap_normalize_labels(section_subtitles, nsections, "section_subtitles")

  section_names <- names(sections)
  if (is.null(section_names) || any(!nzchar(section_names))) {
    section_names <- section_titles
  }

  flat_plots <- .ap_flatten_plot_sections(sections)
  inferred_theme <- .ap_infer_theme_from_plots(flat_plots, fallback = "dark")
  layout_theme <- .ap_get_layout_theme_defaults(inferred_theme)

  if (is.null(section_background)) section_background <- layout_theme$section_background
  if (is.null(section_border))     section_border     <- layout_theme$section_border
  if (is.null(card_background))    card_background    <- layout_theme$card_background
  if (is.null(card_border))        card_border        <- layout_theme$card_border
  if (is.null(card_s ... [truncated]

## 94. 2026-07-04T17:19:28.317Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - user

Prepare the Analytics Shiny App CSS for multiple app themes.

Goal:
Introduce a CSS-variable-based theme foundation so the app can later support Light, Dark, and Pimp modes.

Rules:
- Do not add new UI dependencies.
- Do not add the full settings UI yet unless trivial.
- Do not change app behavior.
- Do not redesign all tabs.
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Tasks:

1. Update www/app.css to use app-level CSS variables.

Define default variables for:
- --aq-bg
- --aq-surface
- --aq-surface-2
- --aq-border
- --aq-text
- --aq-muted
- --aq-primary
- --aq-primary-hover
- --aq-success
- --aq-warning
- --aq-danger
- --aq-radius
- --aq-shadow
- --aq-input-bg
- --aq-input-border
- --aq-nav-active-bg
- --aq-nav-active-text

2. Update existing UI classes to use variables.

Classes likely include:
- aq-page
- aq-card
- aq-section-header
- aq-empty-state
- aq-status-badge
- aq-action-row
- aq-control-group
- aq-preview-panel
- aq-code-panel

3. Add three theme classes.

body.aq-theme-light
body.aq-theme-dark
body.aq-theme-pimp

Light should be the current/default clean style.

Dark should use:
- dark navy/charcoal background
- dark cards
- readable light text
- subtle borders

Pimp should use:
- dark or deep gradient background
- neon accent variables
- slightly stronger card borders/shadows
- but keep input text readable

4. Add a temporary simple way to apply a theme for testing.

Acceptable options:
- hardcode body class in UI for now
- or add a simple selectInput only if easy

Preferred minimal implementation:
- add helper ui_app_shell(..., theme = "light")
- applies class paste0("aq-theme-", theme)

Do not build full persistent app settings yet.

5. Ensure existing Export tab still looks good in light mode.

6. Quick manual theme test:
- apply aq-theme-light
- apply aq-theme-dark
- apply aq-theme-pimp
- confirm Export tab remains readable
- confirm inputs remain readable
- confirm buttons remain visually distinct
- confirm empty state remains readable

## 102. 2026-07-04T17:22:56.662Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - assistant

Implemented the CSS-variable theme foundation.

What changed:
- Added `ui_app_shell(..., theme = "light")` in [R/ui_components.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/ui_components.R).
- Wrapped the app shell in [R/app_ui.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/app_ui.R) with `ui_app_shell(theme = "light")`.
- Updated [www/app.css](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/www/app.css) with app-level variables for surfaces, borders, text, accents, inputs, nav active state, radius, and shadow.
- Added theme classes:
  - `body.aq-theme-light`
  - `body.aq-theme-dark`
  - `body.aq-theme-pimp`
- Converted existing helper-layer classes to use the variables.

Implement the first internal table rendering, theme, and export framework.

Goal:
Create reusable table rendering, table theming, and table export helpers before adding table artifacts.

Rules:
- Do not add table artifacts yet.
- Do not modify AutoPlots.

- Use reactable as the preferred rendering engine if available.
- Use simple htmltools table as fallback if reactable is unavailable.

)

- fallback to html table otherwise

- do not overbuild

Behavior:

- respect light/dark/pimp theme through CSS variables where practical
- good enough for small tables

Add table artifact support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow users to create, preview, organize, display, and export table artifacts using the existing internal table framework.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not use DT.
- Do not create new table rendering logic outside the existing table framework.
- Use render_table() for table previews.
- Use export_table_csv() and export_table_xlsx() for table exports.
- Keep R/ flat. Do not create subdirectories under R/.
- Preserve existing plot and text artifact behavior.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Requirements:

1. Add a Table Artifact creator UI.

Create a section/panel named:

Add Table Block

Inputs:
- table_artifact_label
- table_artifact_section
- table_artifact_type
- table_artifact_vars
- table_artifact_max_rows
- table_artifact_page_size
- table_artifact_theme

table_artifact_type choices:
- Data Preview
- Summary Statistics
- Frequency Table

table_artifact_theme choices:
- auto
- light
- dark
- pimp

Defaults:
- table_artifact_type = "Data Preview"
- table_artifact_max_rows = 25
- table_artifact_page_size = 10
- table_artifact_theme = "auto"

Actions:
- Preview Table
- Add Table Artifact

2. Build table data using existing table builder helpers.

Use:
- build_data_preview_table()
- build_summary_statistics_table()
- build_frequency_table()

Do not duplicate table builder logic in server observers.

3. Preview table artifacts.

Preview Table should:
- build the table data
- render it using render_table()
- show friendly validation errors if no data is uploaded or required variables are missing

4. Create table artifact on Add Table Artifact.

Use create_artifact():

create_artifact(
  artifact_id = next table artifact id, e.g. tbl1, tbl2, tbl3,
  artifact_type = "table",
  label = input$table_artifact_label,
  source_module = "table_builder",
  object = table_data,
  config = list(
    table_type = input$table_artifact_type,
    vars = input$table_artifact_vars,
    max_rows = input$table_artifact_max_rows,
    page_size = input$table_artifact_page_size,
    engine = "reactable",
    theme = input$table_artifact_theme
  ),
  code = generated table builder/render code if currently supported,
  metadata = list(
    n_rows = nrow(table_data),
    n_cols = ncol(table_data)
  ),
  section = input$table_artifact_section,
  order = next order,
  status = "ready"
)

5. Store table artifacts.

If the app already has generalized artifa ... [truncated]

## 110. 2026-07-04T19:18:30.613Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - user

Refine the mixed report display experience in the Layouts page.

Goal:
Make plots, text blocks, and tables render together as a clean report preview with consistent spacing, section headers, artifact cards, and empty states.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not use DT.
- Do not add GenAI.
- Do not add forecasting/modeling modules.
- Do not change artifact creation behavior.
- Do not change project save/load behavior.
- Keep R/ flat. Do not create subdirectories under R/.
- Use existing UI helpers and artifact rendering helpers where possible.
- Keep changes focused on display/report preview quality.

Requirements:

1. Improve section rendering.

For section layout, each section should render as:

- section title
- optional section artifact count or small muted subtitle
- ordered visible artifacts in that section

Use existing UI helpers where possible:
- ui_section_header()
- ui_card()
- ui_empty_state()

2. Improve artifact rendering consistency.

Each artifact in the mixed report preview should have a consistent wrapper.

For each artifact, show:
- artifact label/title
- artifact type badge: Plot / Text / Table
- optional source module
- artifact body

Artifact body:
- plot -> existing plot object
- text -> markdown/text block
- table -> render_table()

3. Avoid double-card clutter.

If render_artifact() already returns a card-like object, do not nest it inside another heavy card.
Use a light wrapper or add an option like:

render_artifact(artifact, chrome = TRUE/FALSE)

If simple, use:
- chrome = TRUE for library preview
- chrome = FALSE inside report section cards

4. Visible artifact filtering.

Layouts should only render artifacts where visible is TRUE.

Hidden artifacts should remain available in Artifact Library but not appear in report preview.

5. Ordering.

Within each section, artifacts should be ordered by order.

Section order should follow first artifact order within each section.

6. Grid layout.

For Grid layout, render visible artifacts in order with consistent card spacing.

Respect layout_cols.

7. Section layout.

For Sections layout, group by section and render ordered artifacts.

Respect layout_cols within each section if already supported.

8. Empty states.

Show clear empty states:
- No artifacts available.
- No visible artifacts selected for this layout.
- This section has no visible artifacts.

9. Theme compatibility.

The report display should look readable in:
- light
- dark
- pimp

Use existing CSS variables/classes.

.aq_class <- function(...) {
  classes <- unlist(list(...), use.names = FALSE)
  classes <- classes[!is.null(classes) & nzchar(classes)]
  paste(classes, collapse = " ")
}

ui_card <- function(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, ..., footer = NULL, class = NULL) {
  tags$section(
    class = .aq_class("aq-card", class),
    if (!is.null(title) || !is.null(subtitle)) {
      tags$header(
        class = "aq-card-header",
        if (!is.null(title)) tags$h3(class = "aq-card-title", title),
        if (!is.null(subtitle)) tags$p(class = "aq-card-subtitle", subtitle)
      )
    },
    tags$div(class = "aq-card-body", ...),
    if (!is.null(footer)) tags$footer(class = "aq-card-footer", footer)
  )
}

ui_app_shell <- function(..., theme = "light") {
  theme <- match.arg(theme, c("light", "dark", "pimp"))

  tagList(
    tags$script(HTML(sprintf(
      "document.body.classList.add('aq-theme-%s');",
      theme
    ))),
    tags$div(class = "aq-app-shell", ...)
  )
}

ui_page <- function(title, subtitle = NULL, ...) {
  tags$div(
    class = "aq-page",
    ui_section_header(title, subtitle),
    ...
  )
}

ui_section_header <- function(title, subtitle = NULL) {
  tags$header(
    class = "aq-section-header",
    tags$h2(class = "aq-section-title", title),
    if (!is.null(subtitle)) tags$p(class = "aq-section-subtitle", subtitle)
  )
}

ui_empty_state <- function(title, message = NULL, icon = NULL) {
  tags$div(
    class = "aq-empty-state",
    if (!is.null(icon)) tags$div(class = "aq-empty-state-icon", icon),
    tags$h3(class = "aq-empty-state-title", title),
    if (!is.null(message)) tags$p(class = "aq-empty-state-message", message)
  )
}

ui_status_badge <- function(
  label,
  status = c("success", "warning", "error", "info", "neutral")
) {
  status <- match.arg(status)

  tags$span(
    class = .aq_class("aq-status-badge", paste0("aq-status-badge-", status)),
    label
  )
}

ui_action_row <- function(...) {
  tags$div(class = "aq-action-row", ...)
}

ui_control_group <- function(title = NULL, ..., description = NULL) {
  tags$section(
    class = "aq-control-group",
    if (!is.null(title)) tags$h3(class = "aq-control-group-title", title),
    if (!is.null(description)) tags$p(class = "aq-control-group-description", description),
    tags$div(class = "aq-control-group-body", ...)
  )
}

ui_preview_panel <- function(title = "Preview", ..., height = NULL) {
  style <- if (!is.null(height)) paste0("min-h ... [truncated]

page_analysis_modules_server <- function(id, ctx) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
    module_result <- reactiveVal(NULL)

    output$eda_column_inputs <- renderUI({
      data <- tryCatch(ctx$uploaded_data(), error = function(e) NULL)
      choices <- if (is.null(data)) character() else names(data)
      numeric_choices <- if (is.null(data)) {
        character()
      } else {
        names(data)[vapply(data, is.numeric, logical(1))]
      }

      tagList(
        selectInput(
          session$ns("eda_univariate_vars"),
          "UnivariateVars",
          choices = choices,
          selected = intersect(c("Spend", "Revenue", "Clicks", "Channel"), choices),
          multiple = TRUE
        ),
        selectInput(
          session$ns("eda_corr_vars"),
          "CorrVars",
          choices = numeric_choices,
          selected = intersect(c("Spend", "Revenue", "Clicks"), numeric_choices),
          multiple = TRUE
        ), ... [truncated]

Create a structured product backlog for the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Capture current completed work, near-term priorities, and future roadmap so development does not sprawl into a Quantico-style everything-app.

Rules:
- Documentation only.
- Do not change app code.
- Do not add dependencies.
- Keep backlog practical and execution-oriented.

Create:
1. docs/product_backlog.md
2. docs/product_backlog.csv

Backlog fields:
- id
- category
- feature
- description
- priority: P0, P1, P2, P3
- phase: Foundation, Report Builder, Analysis Modules, Code Runner, GenAI, UI/UX, Platform, Release
- status: Done, In Progress, Planned, Deferred, Blocked
- depends_on
- source: New App, AutoPlots, AutoQuant, Quantico, New Idea
- risk: Low, Medium, High
- notes

- extracted app from AutoPlots repo
- flat R/ package-like structure
- page modules

- code_run_model.R
- manual local trusted code runner prototype
- output-to-artifact conversion
- code history UI
- report display polish
- Artifact Library polish
- project save/load QA for artifacts/plans/code runs
- Electron smoke test after module additions

Analysis Modules:
- AutoQuant EDA full section support
- Target Analysis adapter
- Model Assessment adapter
- Model Insights adapter
- SHAP Analysis design
- SHAP Analysis module
- CatBoost Builder module
- Forecasting module
- recommended report plans for each module

- data profile object

- GenAI policy object
- proposal object
- permission gate hooks
- plot recommendations
- title/caption generation
- section summaries
- report reviewer

#' @title Target Analysis Report
#'
#' @description
#' Renders an HTML target analysis / model readiness report from a pre-generated
#' artifact object created by `generate_target_analysis_artifacts()`.
#'
#' @author Adrian Antico
#' @family Reports
#'
#' @param artifacts Output object returned by `generate_target_analysis_artifacts()`.
#' @param OutputPath Directory where the rendered HTML report should be saved.
#'   If `NULL`, the current working directory is used.
#' @param DataName Optional report/data name. If `NULL`, attempts to use
#'   `artifacts$metadata$DataName`, then falls back to `"Modeling Data"`.
#' @param Theme Character value passed through to the RMarkdown report.
#' @param OutputFile Optional full output file path. If `NULL`, a file name is
#'   generated as `"TargetAnalysis-{DataName}.html"` inside `OutputPath`.
#'
#' @return Invisibly returns the rendered HTML file path.
#'
#' @details
#' This function does not compute target analysis artifacts. It only renders the
#' report from artifacts that were already generated. This separates evidence
#' generation from report rendering, allowing the same artifact object to be
#' reused for RMarkdown, Shiny, static exports, or LLM/API workflows.
#'
#' Typical usage:
#'
#' \preformatted{
#' artifacts <- generate_target_analysis_artifacts(
#'   data = dt,
#'   DataName = "Modeling Data",
#'   TargetVar = "conversions",
#'   TrendDateVar = "event_date",
#'   TrendGroupVar = c("channel", "market"),
#'   Theme = "dark"
#' )
#'
#' TargetAnalysisReport(
#'   artifacts = artifacts,
#'   OutputPath = "reports"
#' )
#' }
#'
#' @export
TargetAnalysisReport <- function(
    artifacts,
    OutputPath = NULL,
    DataName = NULL,
    Theme = "dark",
    OutputFile = NULL
) {

  if (missing(artifacts) || is.null(artifacts)) {
    stop(
      "`artifacts` must be supplied. Use `generate_target_analysis_artifacts()` first.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  appDir <- system.file("r-markdowns", package = "AutoQuant")

  if (!nzchar(appDir)) {
    stop(
      "Could not find the AutoQuant RMarkdown directory: inst/r-markdowns.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  rmd_file <- file.path(appDir, "Target_Analysis_ ... [truncated]

}
\description{
Generates model assessment artifacts from a data set and target
variable. This function performs the calculations that previously lived
inside the Target Analysis R Markdown report and returns reusable tables,
widgets, plots, metadata, and optional export sidecars.
}
\details{
This function does not call any LLM provider. It only prepares artifacts that
can be rendered by R Markdown, Shiny, Quarto, or passed to an LLM workflow
later.
}
\examples{
\dontrun{
target_artifacts <- generate_model_assessment_artifacts(
  data = dt,
  DataName = "Modeling Data",
  TargetVar = "converted",
  TrendDateVar = "event_date",
  TrendGroupVar = c("channel", "market"),
  Theme = "dark",
  OutputPath = "target_artifacts",
  ExportPNG = TRUE
)
}

}

## Supervised Learning <img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AdrianAntico/AutoQuant/master/Images/SupervisedLearningImage.png" align="right" width="80" />

<details><summary>Expand to view content</summary>
<p>

<br>

### Regression

<details><summary>click to expand</summary>
<p>

<details><summary>Regression Description</summary>
<p>
  
The Auto_Regression() models handle a multitude of tasks. In order:
1. Convert your data to data.table format for faster processing
2. Transform your target variable using the best normalization method based on the <code>AutoTransformationCreate()</code> function
3. Create train, validation, and test data, utilizing the <code>AutoDataPartition()</code> function, if you didn't supply those directly to the function
4. Consoldate columns that are used for modeling and what metadata you want returned in your test data with predictions
5. Dichotomize categorical variables (for <code>AutoXGBoostRegression()</code>) and save the factor levels for scoring in a way that guarentees consistency across training, validation, and test data sets, utilizing the <code>DummifyDT()</code> function
6. Save the final modeling column names for reference
7. Handles the data conversion to the appropriate modeling type, such as CatBoost, H2O, and XGBoost
8. Multi-armed bandit hyperparameter tuning using randomized probability matching, if you choose to grid tune

#' @title ROCPlot
#'
#' @description Internal usage for classification methods. Returns an ROC plot
#'
#' @author Adrian Antico
#' @family Model Evaluation and Interpretation
#'
#' @param data validation data
#' @param TargetName Target variable name
#' @param SavePlot TRUE or FALSE
#' @param Name Name for saving
#' @param metapath Passthrough
#' @param modelpath Passthrough
#'
#' @return ROC Plot for classification models
#' @export
ROCPlot <- function(data = ValidationData,
                    TargetName = TargetColumnName,
                    SavePlot = SaveModelObjects,
                    Name = ModelID,
                    metapath = metadata_path,
                    modelpath = model_path) {

  c1 <- as.numeric(data[,.N])
  c2 <- as.numeric(100000)
  if(!is.null(100000)) if(c1 > c2) temp <- data[order(runif(.N))][seq_len(100000)] else temp <- data
  AUC_Metrics <- pROC::roc(
    response = temp[[eval(TargetName)]],
    predictor = temp[["p1"]],
    na.rm = TRUE,
    algorithm = 3L,
    auc = TRUE,
    ci = TRUE)
  rm(temp)

  # Turn into a data.table
  AUC_Data <- data.table::data.table(
    ModelNumber = 0L,
    Sensitivity = AUC_Metrics$sensitivities,
    Specificity = AUC_Metrics$specificities)

  # Create plot
  ROC_Plot <- eval(ggplot2::ggplot(AUC_Data, ggplot2::aes(x = 1 - Specificity)) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(ggplot2::aes(y = AUC_Data[["Sensitivity"]]), color = "blue") +
    ggplot2::geom_abline(slope = 1, color = "black") +
    ggplot2::labs(title = paste0("Catboost AUC: ", 100 * round(AUC_Metrics$auc, 3), "%"), caption = 'AutoQuant') +
    ChartTheme() + ggplot2::xlab("Specificity") +
    ggplot2::ylab("Sensitivity"))

  # Save plot
  if(SavePlot) {
    if(!is.null(metapath)) {
      ggplot2::ggsave(plot = ROC_Plot, filename = paste0(Name, "_ROC_Plot.png"), path = file.path(metapath))
    } else {
      ggplot2::ggsave(plot = ROC_Plot, filename = paste0(Name, "_ROC_Plot.png"), path = file.path(modelpath))
    }
  }

  # Return
  return(list(ROC_Plot = ROC_Plot, AUC_Metrics = AUC_Metrics))
}

#' @param FacetCols Defaults to 1 which causes no faceting to occur horizontally. Otherwise, supply a numeric value for the number of output grid columns
#' @param FacetLevels Faceting rows x columns is the max number of levels allowed in a grid. If your GroupVar has more you can supply the levels to display.
#' @param Height "400px"
#' @param Width "200px"
#' @param ShowLabels character
#' @param Theme "auritus", "azul", "bee-inspired", "blue", "caravan", "carp", "chalk", "cool", "dark", "dark-blue", "dark-bold", "dark-digerati", "dark-fresh-cut", "dark-mushroom", "eduardo", "essos", "forest", "fresh-cut", "fruit", "gray", "green", "halloween", "helianthus", "infographic", "inspired", "jazz", "london", "macarons", "macarons2", "mint", "purple-passion", "rainbow", "red", "red-velvet", "roma", "royal", "sakura", "shine", "tech-blue", "v5", "vintage", "walden", "wef", "weforum", "westeros", "wonderland"
#' @param TimeLine logical
#' @param MouseScroll logical, zoom via mouse scroll
#' @param SampleSize numeric
#' @param Debug Debugging purposes
#' @return plot
#' @export
ROC <- function(dt = NULL,
                SampleSize = 100000,
                XVar = NULL,
                YVar = NULL,
                GroupVar = NULL,
                YVarTrans = "Identity",
                XVarTrans = "Identity",
                FacetRows = 1,
                FacetCols = 1,
                FacetLevels = NULL,
                AggMethod = 'mean',
                Height = NULL,
                Width = NULL,
                ShowLabels = FALSE,
                Theme = "dark",
                MouseScroll = FALSE,
                TimeLine = FALSE,
                title.text = NULL,
                yAxis.title = NULL,
                xAxis.title = NULL,
                Debug = FALSE) {

  # ROC
  fastROC <- function(preds, target) {
    class_sorted <- target[order(preds, decreasing = TRUE)]
    TPR <- cumsum(class_sorted) / sum(target)
    FPR <- cumsum(class_sorted == 0) / sum(target == 0)
    return(
      list(
        tpr = TPR,
        fpr = FPR
      )
    )
  }

  # AUC
  fastAUC <- function(preds, target) {
    x <- preds
    y <- target
    x1 = x[y == 1]; n1 = length(x1);
    x2 = x[y == 0]; n2 = length(x2);
    r = rank(c(x1,x2))
    auc = (sum(r[1:n1]) - n1 * (n1 + 1) / 2) / n1 / n2
    return(auc)
  }

  if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
    dt <- data ... [truncated]

#' @param MouseScroll logical, zoom via mouse scroll
#' @param AggMethod character
#' @param GroupVar Character variable
#' @param Debug Debugging purposes
#' @return plot
#' @export
PartialDependence.Box <- function(dt = NULL,
                                  PreAgg = TRUE,
                                  SampleSize = 100000L,
                                  XVar = NULL,
                                  YVar = NULL,
                                  ZVar = NULL,
                                  GroupVar = NULL,
                                  YVarTrans = "Identity",
                                  XVarTrans = "Identity",
                                  ZVarTrans = "Identity",
                                  FacetRows = 1,
                                  FacetCols = 1,
                                  FacetLevels = NULL,
                                  NumberBins = 20,
                                  AggMethod = "mean",
                                  Height = NULL,
                                  Width = NULL,
                                  ShowLabels = FALSE,
                                  Theme = "dark",
                                  MouseScroll = FALSE,
                                  EchartsLabels = FALSE,
                                  TimeLine = FALSE,
                                  title.text = NULL,
                                  yAxis.title = NULL,
                                  xAxis.title = NULL,
                                  Debug = FALSE) {

  if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
  })

  GroupVar <- NULL

  # Minimize data before moving on
  if(Debug) print("Plot.PartialDependence.Box # Minimize data before moving on")
  Ncols <- ncol(dt)
  if(Ncols > 3L) {
    dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt[, .SD, .SDcols = c(YVar, XVar, ZVar)])
  } else {
    dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt)
  }

  # If actual is in factor form, convert to numeric
  if(Debug) print("Plot.PartialDependence.Box # If actual is in factor form, convert to numeric")
  if(!is.numeric(dt1[[YVar]])) {
    data.table::set(dt1, j = YVar, value = as.numeric(as.character(dt1[[YVar]])))
  }

  # Add a column that ranks predicted values
  dt1[, eval(XVar) := as.character(round(data.table::frank(get(XVar)) * (NumberBins) / .N) / NumberBins)]
  dt1[, `Target - Predicted` ... [truncated]

.table_as_data_table <- function(data) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(data.table::data.table())
  }

  data.table::as.data.table(data)
}

.format_table_numbers <- function(data, digits = 3) {
  data <- data.table::copy(.table_as_data_table(data))
  numeric_cols <- names(data)[vapply(data, is.numeric, logical(1))]
  for (column in numeric_cols) {
    data[[column]] <- round(data[[column]], digits = digits)
  }

  data
}

render_table <- function(
  data,
  engine = c("reactable", "html"),
  title = NULL,
  subtitle = NULL,
  page_size = 10,
  searchable = TRUE,
  sortable = TRUE,
  filterable = FALSE,
  digits = 3,
  theme = c("auto", "light", "dark", "pimp")
) {
  engine <- match.arg(engine)
  theme <- .normalize_table_theme(theme)

  table <- if (identical(engine, "reactable") &&
               requireNamespace("reactable", quietly = TRUE)) {
    render_reactable_table(
      data = data,
      page_size = page_size,
      searchable = searchable,
      sortable = sortable,
      filterable = filterable,
      digits = digits,
      theme = theme
    )
  } else {
    render_html_table(data = data, digits = digits, theme = theme)
  }

  htmltools::tags$div(
    class = paste("aq-table-wrapper", paste0("aq-table-", theme)),
    if (!is.null(title)) htmltools::tags$h3(class = "aq-table-title", title),
    if (!is.null(subtitle)) htmltools::tags$p(class = "aq-table-subtitle", subtitle),
    table
  )
}

render_reactable_table <- function(
  data,
  page_size = 10,
  searchable = TRUE,
  sortable = TRUE,
  filterable = FALSE,
  digits = 3,
  theme = c("auto", "light", "dark", "pimp")
) {
  if (!requireNamespace("reactable", quietly = TRUE)) {
    return(render_html_table(data = data, digits = digits, theme = theme))
  }

  data <- .table_as_data_table(data)
  numeric_cols <- names(data)[vapply(data, is.numeric, logical(1))]
  columns <- stats::setNames(
    lapply(names(data), function(column) {
      if (column %in% numeric_cols) {
        return(reactable::colDef(format = reactable::colFormat(digits = digits)))
      }

      reactable::colDef()
    }),
    names(data)
  )

  reactable::reactable(
    data,
    columns = columns,
    searchable = searchable,
    sortable = sortable,
    filterable = filterable,
    defaultPageSize = page_size,
    theme = get_reactable_theme(theme),
    class = "aq-table aq-reactable"
  )
}

render_html_table <- function(
  data,
  digits = 3,
  theme = c("auto", ... [truncated]

.aq_class <- function(...) {
  classes <- unlist(list(...), use.names = FALSE)
  classes <- classes[!is.null(classes) & nzchar(classes)]
  paste(classes, collapse = " ")
}

ui_card <- function(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, ..., footer = NULL, class = NULL) {
  tags$section(
    class = .aq_class("aq-card", class),
    if (!is.null(title) || !is.null(subtitle)) {
      tags$header(
        class = "aq-card-header",
        if (!is.null(title)) tags$h3(class = "aq-card-title", title),
        if (!is.null(subtitle)) tags$p(class = "aq-card-subtitle", subtitle)
      )
    },
    tags$div(class = "aq-card-body", ...),
    if (!is.null(footer)) tags$footer(class = "aq-card-footer", footer)
  )
}

ui_app_shell <- function(..., theme = "light") {
  theme <- match.arg(theme, c("light", "dark", "pimp"))

  tagList(
    tags$script(HTML(sprintf(
      "document.body.classList.add('aq-theme-%s');",
      theme
    ))),
    tags$div(class = "aq-app-shell", ...)
  )
}

ui_page <- function(title, subtitle = NULL, ...) {
  tags$div(
    class = "aq-page",
    ui_section_header(title, subtitle),
    ...
  )
}

ui_section_header <- function(title, subtitle = NULL) {
  tags$header(
    class = "aq-section-header",
    tags$h2(class = "aq-section-title", title),
    if (!is.null(subtitle)) tags$p(class = "aq-section-subtitle", subtitle)
  )
}

ui_empty_state <- function(title, message = NULL, icon = NULL) {
  tags$div(
    class = "aq-empty-state",
    if (!is.null(icon)) tags$div(class = "aq-empty-state-icon", icon),
    tags$h3(class = "aq-empty-state-title", title),
    if (!is.null(message)) tags$p(class = "aq-empty-state-message", message)
  )
}

ui_status_badge <- function(
  label,
  status = c("success", "warning", "error", "info", "neutral")
) {
  status <- match.arg(status)

  tags$span(
    class = .aq_class("aq-status-badge", paste0("aq-status-badge-", status)),
    label
  )
}

ui_action_row <- function(...) {
  tags$div(class = "aq-action-row", ...)
}

ui_control_group <- function(title = NULL, ..., description = NULL) {
  tags$section(
    class = "aq-control-group",
    if (!is.null(title)) tags$h3(class = "aq-control-group-title", title),
    if (!is.null(description)) tags$p(class = "aq-control-group-description", description),
    tags$div(class = "aq-control-group-body", ...)
  )
}

## 144. 2026-07-05T02:37:39.403Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - user

Using our forked electron-ifyer package: Run an Electron smoke test checkpoint after Code Runner implementation.

Goal:
Verify that the Analytics Shiny App still works correctly inside the Electron shell now that Code Runner, Code History, rerun/duplicate, and artifact conversion are implemented.

Rules:
- Do not add product features.
- Do not refactor unless a clear Electron-specific issue is found.
- If the issue happens in browser Shiny and Electron, fix AnalyticsShinyApp.
- If the issue happens only in Electron, inspect/fix the Electron wrapper repo.
- Do not modify AutoQuant or AutoPlots unless a clear package integration bug is found.
- Do not use DT.
- Keep R/ flat. Do not create subdirectories under R/.

Test areas:

1. Startup
- Launch the app through Electron.
- Confirm R/Shiny backend starts.
- Confirm Electron window loads the app.
- Confirm app closes cleanly.
- Confirm no orphaned R process remains if practical.

2. Navigation
Confirm pages load:
- Data
- Plot Builder
- Artifact Library
- Layouts
- Analysis Modules
- Code Runner
- Export
- Project
- Settings if present

3. Data + artifacts
- Upload sample CSV.
- Build a Line plot.
- Add plot artifact.
- Add text artifact.
- Add table artifact.
- Confirm Artifact Library shows all.
- Confirm Layouts render mixed artifacts.

4. Analysis modules
Run or smoke:
- AutoQuant EDA
- AutoQuant Model Assessment if fixture/config available
- AutoQuant Regression Model Insights if fixture/config available
- AutoQuant Binary Model Insights if fixture/config available

At minimum, run QA helpers if available:
- qa_autoquant_eda_integration()
- qa_autoquant_model_assessment_integration()
- qa_autoquant_regression_model_insights_integration()
- qa_autoquant_binary_model_insights_integration()
- qa_analysis_modules_integration()

5. Report plans
- Run AutoQuant EDA.
- Confirm artifacts generated.
- Confirm report plans generated.
- Preview plan.
- Apply plan.
- Confirm Layouts render applied plan.

6. Code Runner
Default safety:
- Confirm code execution is disabled by default.
- Confirm Run Code is blocked by default.

Trusted local execution:
- Enable local_trusted execution.
- Enable manual code.
- Run: 1 + 1
- Confirm success result.
- Run code with warning.
- Confirm warning captured.
- Run code with stop("boom").
- Confirm error captured.
- Run blocked function example.
- Confirm blocked.

Code History:
- Confirm run appears in history.
- Select run and view details.
- Duplicate run.
- Rerun selected.
- C ... [truncated]

<!-- README.md is generated from README.qmd. Please edit that file -->

# shinyelectron <a href="https://r-pkg.thecoatlessprofessor.com/shinyelectron/" alt="shinyelectron"><picture><source media="(prefers-color-scheme: dark)" srcset="man/figures/logo-shinyelectron-light-animated.svg"><img src="man/figures/logo-shinyelectron-dark-animated.svg" width="170" align="right" alt="A hexagonal logo for shinyelectron"/></picture></a>

<!-- badges: start -->

[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/coatless-rpkg/shinyelectron/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/coatless-rpkg/shinyelectron/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
<img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Status-Prototype-orange" alt="Prototype">
<img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Status-Experimental-blue" alt="Experimental">
<!-- badges: end -->

Turn any Shiny app (R or Python) into a standalone desktop application
that runs on macOS, Windows, and Linux. No web server, no browser tab,
no deployment infrastructure. Just an `.app`, `.exe`, or AppImage your
users double-click to open.

<picture><source srcset="man/figures/pipeline-overview-dark.svg" media="(prefers-color-scheme: dark)" /><img src="man/figures/pipeline-overview.svg" alt="pipeline overview" /></picture>

> [!IMPORTANT]
>
> This package is currently in the prototype/experimental stage. It is
> not yet on CRAN and may have rough edges. **Not recommended for
> production applications at this time.**

## Private and local R packages

R packages do not need to be published on CRAN to ship with a desktop app.
With `runtime_strategy = "bundled"`, shinyelectron can install packages
directly from GitHub or the build machine and embed them in the app's private R
library. End users do not need R, the original package directory, repository
access, or credentials. Bundling is distribution, not source-code protection:
installed package contents remain inspectable by a determined user.

For example, a Windows user might keep an app and two unpublished packages in
one project:

``` text
C:/Users/alex/Documents/acme-desktop/
|-- shiny-app/
|   |-- app.R
|   `-- _shinyelectron.yml
|-- packages/
|   |-- acmeData/
|   |   |-- DESCRIPTION
|   |   `-- R/
|   `-- acmeReports_2.1.0.tar.gz
`-- builds/
```

The fake `shiny-app/_shinyelectron.yml` would look like this:

``` yaml
dependencies:
  r:
    packages:
      # Public GitHub repository; @ref may be a branch, tag, or commit ... [truncated]

<!-- README.md is generated from README.qmd. Please edit that file -->

# shinyelectron <a href="https://r-pkg.thecoatlessprofessor.com/shinyelectron/" alt="shinyelectron"><picture><source media="(prefers-color-scheme: dark)" srcset="man/figures/logo-shinyelectron-light-animated.svg"><img src="man/figures/logo-shinyelectron-dark-animated.svg" width="170" align="right" alt="A hexagonal logo for shinyelectron"/></picture></a>

<!-- badges: start -->

[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/coatless-rpkg/shinyelectron/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/coatless-rpkg/shinyelectron/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
<img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Status-Prototype-orange" alt="Prototype">
<img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Status-Experimental-blue" alt="Experimental">
<!-- badges: end -->

Turn any Shiny app (R or Python) into a standalone desktop application
that runs on macOS, Windows, and Linux. No web server, no browser tab,
no deployment infrastructure. Just an `.app`, `.exe`, or AppImage your
users double-click to open.

<picture><source srcset="man/figures/pipeline-overview-dark.svg" media="(prefers-color-scheme: dark)" /><img src="man/figures/pipeline-overview.svg" alt="pipeline overview" /></picture>

> [!IMPORTANT]
>
> This package is currently in the prototype/experimental stage. It is
> not yet on CRAN and may have rough edges. **Not recommended for
> production applications at this time.**

## Private and local R packages

R packages do not need to be published on CRAN to ship with a desktop app.
With `runtime_strategy = "bundled"`, shinyelectron can install packages
directly from GitHub or the build machine and embed them in the app's private R
library. End users do not need R, the original package directory, repository
access, or credentials. Bundling is distribution, not source-code protection:
installed package contents remain inspectable by a determined user.

For example, a Windows user might keep an app and two unpublished packages in
one project:

``` text
C:/Users/alex/Documents/acme-desktop/
|-- shiny-app/
|   |-- app.R
|   `-- _shinyelectron.yml
|-- packages/
|   |-- acmeData/
|   |   |-- DESCRIPTION
|   |   `-- R/
|   `-- acmeReports_2.1.0.tar.gz
`-- builds/
```

The fake `shiny-app/_shinyelectron.yml` would look like this:

``` yaml
dependencies:
  r:
    packages:
      # Public GitHub repository; @ref may be a branch, tag, or commit ... [truncated]

id_cols <- setdiff(names(dt), c(target, features))

# Run function
ModelObject <- AutoQuant::AutoCatBoostRegression(

  # GPU or CPU and the number of available GPUs
  task_type = 'GPU',
  NumGPUs = 1,
  NumOfParDepPlots = 0,

  # Metadata args
  OutputSelection = c('Importances','EvalMetrics','Score_TrainData'),
  ModelID = 'Test_Model_1',
  model_path = getwd(),
  metadata_path = getwd(),
  ReturnModelObjects = TRUE,

  # Data args
  data = dt,
  TargetColumnName = "conversions",
  FeatureColNames = features,
  IDcols = id_cols,
  TransformNumericColumns = NULL,
  Methods = c('Asinh','Asin','Log','LogPlus1','Sqrt','Logit'),
  Trees = 200
)

# ----------------------------
# 5. Run artifact generator
# ----------------------------

ModelInsightsArtifacts <- generate_regression_model_insights_artifacts(

  # Model object from AutoCatBoostRegression()
  ModelObject = ModelObject,

  # Important for CatBoost-specific importance / interaction handling
  Algo = "catboost",

  # Explicitly pass these so the generator does not have to infer
  TargetColumnName = target,
  PredictionColumnName = "Predict",
  FeatureColumnNames = features,

  # Good candidates for segment and Simpson's Paradox / by-variable checks
  SegmentVars = c("channel", "region", "customer_segment"),
  ByVars = c("channel", "region", "customer_segment"),

  # Optional, only use this if one of your columns is truly a date/time var
  DateVar = NULL,

  # Optional ID columns retained in top-error tables
  IDColumn = if (length(id_cols) > 0L) id_cols[1L] else NULL,

  # Visual theme
  Theme = "dark",

  # First run: keep everything dynamic, no exports
  OutputPath = NULL
)

ReportPath <- RegressionModelInsightsReport(
  artifacts = ModelInsightsArtifacts,
  OutputPath = getwd(),
  OutputFile = "Regression_ModelInsights_Report.html",
  Quiet = FALSE
)

# @noRd
.ap_get_plot_theme <- function(p) {
  theme <- tryCatch(p$x$theme, error = function(e) NULL)

  if (
    !is.null(theme) &&
    is.character(theme) &&
    length(theme) == 1L &&
    !is.na(theme) &&
    nzchar(theme)
  ) {
    return(theme)
  }

  NULL
}

# @noRd
.ap_infer_theme_from_plots <- function(plots, fallback = "dark") {
  themes <- vapply(
    plots,
    function(p) {
      theme <- .ap_get_plot_theme(p)
      if (is.null(theme)) NA_character_ else theme
    },
    character(1L)
  )

  themes <- themes[!is.na(themes) & nzchar(themes)]

  if (!length(themes)) {
    return(fallback)
  }

  unique_themes <- unique(themes)

  if (length(unique_themes) > 1L) {
    warning(
      "Multiple plot themes detected: ",
      paste(unique_themes, collapse = ", "),
      ". Using the first detected theme: ",
      unique_themes[1L],
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  unique_themes[1L]
}

# @noRd
.ap_is_nonempty_chr <- function(x) {
  !is.null(x) && length(x) == 1L && !is.na(x) && nzchar(x)
}

# @noRd
.ap_css <- function(...) {
  vals <- list(...)
  vals <- vals[!vapply(vals, is.null, logical(1))]
  paste0(unlist(vals, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "")
}

# @noRd
.ap_validate_plot_list <- function(plots, arg = "plots") {
  if (!is.list(plots)) {
    stop(sprintf("`%s` must be a list of echarts4r plots, htmlwidgets, or htmltools tags.", arg), call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (length(plots) == 0L) {
    stop(sprintf("`%s` must contain at least one plot.", arg), call. = FALSE)
  }

  invisible(TRUE)
}

# @noRd
.ap_normalize_labels <- function(x, n, arg) {
  if (is.null(x)) {
    return(rep(NA_character_, n))
  }

  if (!is.character(x) || length(x) != n) {
    stop(sprintf("`%s` must be NULL or a character vector with length equal to the number of plots.", arg), call. = FALSE)
  }

  x
}

# @noRd
.ap_card_header <- function(
    title = NA_character_,
    subtitle = NA_character_,
    title_color = "#E5E7EB",
    subtitle_color = "#9CA3AF",
    title_size = "16px",
    subtitle_size = "13px",
    font_family = "Segoe UI, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif"
) {
  children <- list()

  if (!is.na(title) && nzchar(title)) {
    children[[length(children) + 1L]] <- htmltools::tags$div(
      style = paste0(
        "font-family:", font_family, ";",
        "font-size:", title_size, ";",
        "font-weight:700;",
        "color:", title_color, ";",
        "margin:0 0 4px 0;"
      ), ... [truncated]

#' @param card_padding CSS padding inside cards.
#' @param card_shadow CSS box-shadow for cards.
#' @param title_color CSS color for card titles.
#' @param subtitle_color CSS color for card subtitles.
#' @param title_size CSS font-size for card titles.
#' @param subtitle_size CSS font-size for card subtitles.
#' @param font_family CSS font-family used for titles/subtitles.
#' @param container_class CSS class for each plot container.
#' @param grid_class CSS class for the grid layout container.
#' @param background Optional CSS background for the full grid wrapper.
#' @param width CSS width of the full grid wrapper.
#' @param save_path Optional file path to save standalone HTML.
#' @param selfcontained Logical passed to htmlwidgets::saveWidget.
#' @param return_code Logical. If TRUE, return list(html = ..., code = ...).
#'
#' @return A browsable HTML grid, or list(html, code) when return_code = TRUE.
#'
#' @export
display_plots_grid <- function(
    plots,
    cols = NULL,
    titles = NULL,
    subtitles = NULL,
    gap = "20px",
    padding = "0px",
    min_width = "350px",
    last_row = c("span", "left", "center", "none"),
    card = TRUE,
    card_background = NULL,
    card_border = NULL,
    card_radius = "14px",
    card_padding = "14px",
    card_shadow = NULL,
    title_color = NULL,
    subtitle_color = NULL,
    title_size = "16px",
    subtitle_size = "13px",
    font_family = "Segoe UI, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif",
    container_class = "plot-card",
    grid_class = "plot-grid",
    background = NULL,
    width = "100%",
    save_path = NULL,
    selfcontained = TRUE,
    return_code = FALSE
) {

  .ap_validate_plot_list(plots)

  nplots <- length(plots)
  last_row <- match.arg(last_row)

  explicit_cols <- !is.null(cols)

  if (explicit_cols) {
    if (!(is.numeric(cols) && length(cols) == 1L && is.finite(cols) && cols >= 1)) {
      stop("`cols` must be a single positive integer or NULL.", call. = FALSE)
    }
    cols <- as.integer(cols)
  }

  titles <- .ap_normalize_labels(titles, nplots, "titles")
  subtitles <- .ap_normalize_labels(subtitles, nplots, "subtitles")

  inferred_theme <- .ap_infer_theme_from_plots(plots, fallback = "dark")
  layout_theme <- .ap_get_layout_theme_defaults(inferred_theme)

  if (is.null(card_background)) card_background <- layout_theme$card_background
  if (is.null(card_border))     card_border     <- layout_theme$card_borde ... [truncated]

#' @param return_code Logical. If TRUE, return list(html = ..., code = ...).
#'
#' @return A browsable HTML tab set, or list(html, code) when return_code = TRUE.
#'
#' @export
display_plots_tabs <- function(
    plots,
    tab_titles = NULL,
    titles = NULL,
    subtitles = NULL,
    selected = 1L,
    card = TRUE,
    card_background = NULL,
    card_border = NULL,
    card_radius = "14px",
    card_padding = "14px",
    card_shadow = NULL,
    title_color = NULL,
    subtitle_color = NULL,
    title_size = "16px",
    subtitle_size = "13px",
    font_family = "Segoe UI, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif",
    tabs_class = "plot-tabs",
    save_path = NULL,
    selfcontained = TRUE,
    return_code = FALSE
) {

  .ap_validate_plot_list(plots)

  nplots <- length(plots)

  if (is.null(tab_titles)) {
    nms <- names(plots)
    if (!is.null(nms) && all(nzchar(nms))) {
      tab_titles <- nms
    } else {
      tab_titles <- paste0("Plot ", seq_len(nplots))
    }
  }

  tab_titles <- .ap_normalize_labels(tab_titles, nplots, "tab_titles")
  titles <- .ap_normalize_labels(titles, nplots, "titles")
  subtitles <- .ap_normalize_labels(subtitles, nplots, "subtitles")

  inferred_theme <- .ap_infer_theme_from_plots(plots, fallback = "dark")
  layout_theme <- .ap_get_layout_theme_defaults(inferred_theme)

  if (is.null(card_background)) card_background <- layout_theme$card_background
  if (is.null(card_border))     card_border     <- layout_theme$card_border
  if (is.null(card_shadow))     card_shadow     <- layout_theme$card_shadow
  if (is.null(title_color))     title_color     <- layout_theme$title_color
  if (is.null(subtitle_color))  subtitle_color  <- layout_theme$subtitle_color

  tab_background <- layout_theme$tab_background
  tab_border <- layout_theme$tab_border
  tab_active_background <- layout_theme$tab_active_background
  tab_active_border <- layout_theme$tab_active_border
  tab_text_color <- layout_theme$tab_text_color

  if (!(is.numeric(selected) && length(selected) == 1L && selected >= 1L && selected <= nplots)) {
    stop("`selected` must be a single integer between 1 and length(plots).", call. = FALSE)
  }
  selected <- as.integer(selected)

  uid <- paste0("ap_tabs_", as.integer(stats::runif(1L, 1e6, 9e6)))

  base_card_style <- .ap_card_style(
    card = card,
    card_background = card_background,
    card_border = card_border,
    card_radius = card_radius, ... [truncated]
