#' Fit AutoNLS vNext models with one workflow
#'
#' @param data A data.frame or data.table.
#' @param x Predictor column name.
#' @param y Target column name.
#' @param models Character vector of model names, or "all".
#' @param weights_col Optional non-negative weights column.
#' @param loss One of "mse", "mae", "huber", or "quantile".
#' @param n_starts Number of deterministic starts per model.
#' @param seed Random seed used for start generation.
#' @param optimizer One of "optim" or "nlsLM". `nlsLM` is used only when available.
#' @param quantile_level Quantile used when loss = "quantile".
#' @param huber_delta Delta used when loss = "huber".
#' @param lower_bounds Optional named vector/list overriding lower bounds.
#' @param upper_bounds Optional named vector/list overriding upper bounds.
#' @param maxit Maximum optimizer iterations.
#' @param reltol Relative tolerance for `optim`.
#' @param scale_x Logical. Fit models on scaled x.
#' @param scale_y Logical. Fit models on scaled y and back-transform predictions.
#' @param model_status One of "stable", "experimental", or "all".
#' @param validation_fraction Optional holdout fraction between 0 and 0.5.
#' @param validation_seed Seed used for validation split.
#' @param theme Plot theme metadata retained on the fit object.
#' @return An AutoNLSFit R6 object.
#' @export
AutoNLS <- function(
  data,
  x,
  y,
  models = c("Hill", "Logistic", "Gompertz"),
  weights_col = NULL,
  loss = c("mse", "mae", "huber", "quantile"),
  n_starts = 25,
  seed = 42,
  optimizer = c("optim", "nlsLM"),
  quantile_level = 0.5,
  huber_delta = 1,
  lower_bounds = NULL,
  upper_bounds = NULL,
  maxit = 5000,
  reltol = 1e-8,
  scale_x = TRUE,
  scale_y = TRUE,
  model_status = c("stable", "experimental", "all"),
  validation_fraction = 0,
  validation_seed = 42,
  theme = "dark"
) {
  fit <- AutoNLSFit$new(
    data = data,
    x = x,
    y = y,
    models = models,
    weights_col = weights_col,
    loss = match.arg(loss),
    n_starts = n_starts,
    seed = seed,
    optimizer = match.arg(optimizer),
    quantile_level = quantile_level,
    huber_delta = huber_delta,
    lower_bounds = lower_bounds,
    upper_bounds = upper_bounds,
    maxit = maxit,
    reltol = reltol,
    scale_x = scale_x,
    scale_y = scale_y,
    model_status = match.arg(model_status),
    validation_fraction = validation_fraction,
    validation_seed = validation_seed, ... [truncated]

# Prediction Surface / Contribution Analysis Contract

## Scope

AutoQuant Prediction Surface / Contribution Analysis is a reusable analytical framework for understanding how model predictions change across variables, observations, interactions, groups, and time.

SHAP is the first contribution backend. It is not the conceptual center.

The framework must support:

- static reports
- dashboards
- Shiny applications
- APIs
- scheduled monitoring
- LLM agents
- future AutoQuant analytical modules

Reports are renderers only. They must never recompute analytical artifacts.

This contract covers the long-term framework plus the first convenience wrappers:

- `PredictionSurfaceAnalysis()`
- `RegressionShapAnalysis()`
- `BinaryClassificationShapAnalysis()`

Current transitional generator names may remain while the implementation migrates:

- `generate_regression_shap_analysis_artifacts()`
- `generate_binary_classification_shap_analysis_artifacts()`

Report wrappers follow the standard rendering API:

```r
Report(
  artifact_result = NULL,
  data = NULL,
  OutputPath = getwd(),
  OutputFile = NULL,
  Title = NULL,
  Subtitle = NULL,
  Theme = "dark",
  Open = FALSE,
  Quiet = TRUE,
  ...
)
```

## 1. User Intent

### Core Intent

The user wants to understand the model prediction surface:

- Which variables materially influence predictions?
- How does each variable influence predictions?
- Are effects linear, nonlinear, monotonic, thresholded, saturated, unstable, or segment-specific?
- Which feature values increase or decrease predictions?
- Which observations are driven by which variables?
- Which feature interactions materially change model behavior?
- Do effects differ across groups, time windows, prediction bands, or error bands?
- Are explanations stable enough to trust?
- Are explanations changing between reference data and current data?
- Are there warnings, pitfalls, or misleading interpretations?
- What should the analyst investigate next?

### Regression Intent

For regression, the user wants to understand contributions to a continuous prediction:

- prediction magnitude
- signed contribution direction and size
- residual and error context
- over-prediction and under-prediction drivers
- decomposition from baseline to prediction
- drift in prediction-surface behavior over groups and time

### Binary Classification Intent

For binary classification, the user wants to understand contributions toward ... [truncated]

# Report API Contract

## Principle

Artifact generators own analytical parameters.

Report functions own rendering parameters.

This keeps AutoQuant workflows copy/paste friendly and prevents report wrappers from becoming duplicated analytical APIs.

## Preferred Workflow

Generate artifacts first:

```r
artifact_result <- generate_regression_shap_analysis_artifacts(
  data = scored_data,
  target_col = "Target",
  prediction_col = "Predict",
  DateVar = "Date",
  ByVars = "Segment"
)
```

Render the artifacts:

```r
RegressionShapAnalysisReport(
  artifact_result = artifact_result,
  OutputPath = "reports",
  OutputFile = "regression_shap.html"
)
```

## Convenience Workflow

Report functions may call the matching generator through `data + ...`:

```r
RegressionShapAnalysisReport(
  data = scored_data,
  OutputPath = "reports",
  OutputFile = "regression_shap.html",
  target_col = "Target",
  prediction_col = "Predict",
  DateVar = "Date",
  ByVars = "Segment"
)
```

This is a convenience wrapper. The analytical arguments still belong to the generator.

## Standard Report Signature

Modern report functions should follow this shape:

```r
SomeReport <- function(
  artifact_result = NULL,
  data = NULL,
  OutputPath = getwd(),
  OutputFile = NULL,
  Title = NULL,
  Subtitle = NULL,
  Theme = "dark",
  Open = FALSE,
  Quiet = TRUE,
  ...
)
```

Report functions may support `SelfContained`, `Clean`, `Envir`, `TemplatePath`, `RmdFile`, and `Package` through `...` when needed by the rendering system.

## Behavior

If `artifact_result` is supplied:

- render it directly
- do not call the artifact generator
- ignore analytical arguments in `...`
- avoid recomputation

If `artifact_result` is `NULL`:

- require `data` or another generator-supported data input
- call the matching `generate_*_artifacts()` function
- pass analytical arguments through `...`
- render the generated artifacts

## Modern Report Wrappers

Current modern wrappers should follow this contract:

- `EDAReport()`
- `TargetAnalysisReport()` during the Model Readiness naming transition
- `RegressionModelInsightsReport()`
- `BinaryClassificationModelInsightsReport()`
- `RegressionShapAnalysisReport()`
- `BinaryClassificationShapAnalysisReport()`

`ModelInsightsReport()` is legacy compatibility only and should not shape new report APIs.

## QA Invariants

Report API QA should verify:

- report signatures use the standard shape
- artifact-resu ... [truncated]

.aq_class <- function(...) {
  classes <- unlist(list(...), use.names = FALSE)
  classes <- classes[!is.null(classes) & nzchar(classes)]
  paste(classes, collapse = " ")
}

ui_card <- function(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, ..., footer = NULL, class = NULL) {
  tags$section(
    class = .aq_class("aq-card", class),
    if (!is.null(title) || !is.null(subtitle)) {
      tags$header(
        class = "aq-card-header",
        if (!is.null(title)) tags$h3(class = "aq-card-title", title),
        if (!is.null(subtitle)) tags$p(class = "aq-card-subtitle", subtitle)
      )
    },
    tags$div(class = "aq-card-body", ...),
    if (!is.null(footer)) tags$footer(class = "aq-card-footer", footer)
  )
}

ui_app_shell <- function(..., theme = "light") {
  theme <- match.arg(theme, c("light", "dark", "pimp"))

  tagList(
    tags$script(HTML(sprintf(
      "document.body.classList.add('aq-theme-%s');",
      theme
    ))),
    tags$div(class = "aq-app-shell", ...)
  )
}

ui_page <- function(title, subtitle = NULL, ...) {
  tags$div(
    class = "aq-page",
    ui_section_header(title, subtitle),
    ...
  )
}

ui_section_header <- function(title, subtitle = NULL) {
  tags$header(
    class = "aq-section-header",
    tags$h2(class = "aq-section-title", title),
    if (!is.null(subtitle)) tags$p(class = "aq-section-subtitle", subtitle)
  )
}

ui_empty_state <- function(title, message = NULL, icon = NULL) {
  tags$div(
    class = "aq-empty-state",
    if (!is.null(icon)) tags$div(class = "aq-empty-state-icon", icon),
    tags$h3(class = "aq-empty-state-title", title),
    if (!is.null(message)) tags$p(class = "aq-empty-state-message", message)
  )
}

ui_status_badge <- function(
  label,
  status = c("success", "warning", "error", "info", "neutral")
) {
  status <- match.arg(status)

  tags$span(
    class = .aq_class("aq-status-badge", paste0("aq-status-badge-", status)),
    label
  )
}

ui_action_row <- function(...) {
  tags$div(class = "aq-action-row", ...)
}

ui_control_group <- function(title = NULL, ..., description = NULL) {
  tags$section(
    class = "aq-control-group",
    if (!is.null(title)) tags$h3(class = "aq-control-group-title", title),
    if (!is.null(description)) tags$p(class = "aq-control-group-description", description),
    tags$div(class = "aq-control-group-body", ...)
  )
}

  style <- if (!is.null(height)) paste0("min-h ... [truncated]

## 250. 2026-07-08T03:26:49.194Z - current_projectless_megathread - assistant

I checked the README: valid examples include `dark`, `chalk`, `walden`, etc.; `light` is not listed. The gallery no longer passes `Theme` at all, so it now inherits the AutoPlots default instead of forcing an invalid value. I also noticed other AnalyticsShinyApp modules still default to `"light"`, but I’m leaving that broader app cleanup separate unless you want me to sweep it.


## 251. 2026-07-08T03:27:00.736Z - current_projectless_megathread - assistant

The regenerated screenshots are no longer empty in the sampled high-risk cases I checked: 60-bar, top-50 variable importance, and large heatmap all render as dark-theme AutoPlots widgets. The large-label overlap/truncation is still present in some cases, but that’s now genuine sizing evidence rather than an artifact from an invalid theme.


## 252. 2026-07-08T03:27:09.605Z - current_projectless_megathread - assistant

Yes, I was incorrectly passing `Theme = "light"` in `qa_plot_sizing_gallery()`. You’re right: AutoPlots README does not list `"light"` as a valid theme, and production-style usage should leave `Theme` unnamed so AutoPlots defaults to `"dark"`.

I removed the forced theme and regenerated the gallery:
- `31/31 success`
- `29` PNGs regenerated
- DOCX regenerated, size `1,124,245` bytes
- No `Theme = "light"` in the gallery code/HTML
- No `AutoPlots::Plot.*`
- No `ERR_INVALID_URL`

I spot-checked high-risk screenshots: 60-bar, top-50 variable importance, and large heatmap now render as dark-theme AutoPlots widgets, not empty plots. The missing/overlapping labels at large category counts still show up, but that is now legitimate sizing-gallery evidence for the future static sizing policy.

#' @param Height "400px"
#' @param Width "200px"
#' @param ShowLabels character

#' @param TimeLine logical
#' @param Debug Debugging purposes
#' @return plot
#' @export
VariableImportance <- function(dt = NULL,
                               XVar = NULL,
                               YVar = NULL,
                               GroupVar = NULL,
                               YVarTrans = "Identity",
                               XVarTrans = "Identity",
                               FacetRows = 1,
                               FacetCols = 1,
                               FacetLevels = NULL,
                               AggMethod = 'mean',
                               Height = NULL,
                               Width = NULL,
                               ShowLabels = FALSE,
                               Theme = "dark",
                               TimeLine = FALSE,
                               title.text = "Variable Importance",
                               yAxis.title = "",
                               xAxis.title = "",
                               Debug = FALSE) {

  if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
  })

  # Plot
  dt <- dt[order(Importance)]
  Var <- names(which(unlist(lapply(dt, is.character))))
  Var2 <- names(which(unlist(lapply(dt, is.numeric))))[1L]
  if(length(Var) == 0L) {
    Var <- names(which(unlist(lapply(dt, is.factor))))
    dt[, eval(Var) := as.character(get(Var))]
  }
  dt <- dt[!is.na(get(YVar))]

  p1 <- Bar(
    dt = dt,
    XVar = Var,
    YVar = Var2,
    Theme = Theme,
    MouseScroll = FALSE,
    TimeLine = TimeLine,
    Height = Height,
    Width = Width,
    title.text = title.text,
    yAx ... [truncated]

binary_model_insights_build_feature_plots <- function(scored, feature_summary, target_col, prediction_col, max_features = 5L) {
  plots <- list()
  if (!nrow(feature_summary)) {
    return(plots)
  }

  for (feature in utils::head(feature_summary$feature, max_features)) {
    feature_dt <- data.table::copy(scored)
    if (is.numeric(feature_dt[[feature]]) || is.integer(feature_dt[[feature]])) {
      feature_dt[, feature_bin := cut(
        get(feature),
        breaks = unique(stats::quantile(get(feature), probs = seq(0, 1, length.out = 11L), na.rm = TRUE)),
        include.lowest = TRUE,
        labels = FALSE
      )]
      pdp <- feature_dt[!is.na(feature_bin), .(
        feature_value = mean(get(feature), na.rm = TRUE),
        observed_rate = mean(actual_binary, na.rm = TRUE),
        mean_prediction = mean(prediction, na.rm = TRUE)
      ), by = feature_bin][order(feature_bin)]
      plots[[paste0(feature, "_line")]] <- AutoPlots::PartialDependence.Line(
        dt = scored,
        XVar = feature,
        YVar = "actual_binary",
        ZVar = "prediction",
        Theme = "dark",
        title.text = paste("Partial Dependence Line:", feature),
        xAxis.title = feature,
        yAxis.title = "Actual / Predicted"
      )
      plots[[paste0(feature, "_box")]] <- AutoPlots::PartialDependence.Box(
        dt = scored,
        XVar = feature,
        YVar = "actual_binary",
        ZVar = "prediction",
        Theme = "dark",
        title.text = paste("Partial Dependence Box:", feature),
        xAxis.title = feature,
        yAxis.title = "Target - Predicted"
      )
    } else {
      pdp <- feature_dt[, .(
        n = .N,
        observed_rate = mean(actual_binary, na.rm = TRUE),
        mean_prediction = mean(prediction, na.rm = TRUE)
      ), by = feature][order(-n)][seq_len(min(.N, 20L))]
      pdp[, metric := "Observed Rate"]
      plots[[paste0(feature, "_heatmap")]] <- AutoPlots::HeatMap(
        dt = pdp,
        PreAgg = TRUE,
        XVar = feature,
        YVar = "metric",
        ZVar = "observed_rate",
        Theme = "dark",
        title.text = paste("Partial Dependence Heatmap:", feature),
        xAxis.title = feature,
        yAxis.title = NULL,
        xAxis.axisLabel.rotate = 45,
        tooltip.show = aq_report_dense_axis_tooltip(pdp, feature, "metric")
      )
    }
  }

  plots
}

.table_as_data_table <- function(data) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(data.table::data.table())
  }

  data.table::as.data.table(data)
}

.format_table_numbers <- function(data, digits = 3) {
  data <- data.table::copy(.table_as_data_table(data))
  numeric_cols <- names(data)[vapply(data, is.numeric, logical(1))]
  for (column in numeric_cols) {
    data[[column]] <- round(data[[column]], digits = digits)
  }

  data
}

aq_reactable_exclusion_filter <- function() {
  if (!requireNamespace("htmlwidgets", quietly = TRUE)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  htmlwidgets::JS("
    function(rows, columnId, filterValue) {
      if (!filterValue) return rows;

      const filter = String(filterValue).toLowerCase().trim();
      const exclude = filter.startsWith('!') || filter.startsWith('-');
      const term = exclude ? filter.slice(1).trim() : filter;

      if (!term) return rows;

      return rows.filter(function(row) {
        const rawValue = row.values[columnId];
        const value = rawValue == null ? '' : String(rawValue).toLowerCase();
        const match = value.includes(term);
        return exclude ? !match : match;
      });
    }
  ")
}

aq_reactable_is_text_like <- function(x) {
  is.character(x) || is.factor(x) || is.logical(x)
}

aq_reactable_column_defs <- function(data, digits = 3) {
  data <- .table_as_data_table(data)
  numeric_cols <- names(data)[vapply(data, is.numeric, logical(1))]
  text_cols <- names(data)[vapply(data, aq_reactable_is_text_like, logical(1))]
  exclusion_filter <- aq_reactable_exclusion_filter()

  stats::setNames(
    lapply(names(data), function(column) {
      if (column %in% numeric_cols) {
        return(reactable::colDef(format = reactable::colFormat(digits = digits)))
      }

      if (column %in% text_cols && !is.null(exclusion_filter)) {
        return(reactable::colDef(filterMethod = exclusion_filter))
      }

      reactable::colDef()
    }),
    names(data)
  )
}

render_table <- function(
  data,
  engine = c("reactable", "html"),
  title = NULL,
  subtitle = NULL,
  page_size = 10,
  searchable = TRUE,
  sortable = TRUE,
  filterable = FALSE,
  digits = 3,
  theme = c("auto", "light", "dark", "pimp")
) {
  engine <- match.arg(engine)
  theme <- .normalize_table_theme(theme)

  table <- if (identical(engine, "reactable") &&
               requireNamespace("reactable", quietly = TRUE)) {
    render_reactable_table(
      data = data,
      page_size = pa ... [truncated]

# Task: Design and Implement the AnalyticsShinyApp UX/UI Architecture

## Background

The backend architecture has reached a mature state.

Implemented:

- Artifact Model
- Render Targets
- Project Artifact Collector
- Artifact Quality Policy
- Table Artifact Architecture
- Producer Semantics
- Workflow Integration
- Comprehensive QA

The application is entering a new phase.

The focus is no longer architecture.

The focus is User Experience.

This is **not** a theme refresh.

This is a UX architecture task.

The goal is to make AnalyticsShinyApp as enjoyable to use as AutoPlots is to look at and as enjoyable as the generated RMarkdown reports are to read.

The application should become the "mission control" for the entire analytics workflow.

---

# Primary Objective

Design a UX/UI architecture that optimizes:

- ergonomics
- discoverability
- efficiency
- aesthetics
- consistency
- workflow clarity

while preserving the application's philosophy:

Simple by default.

Extremely powerful when desired.

---

# Guiding Philosophy

The application should satisfy three user types simultaneously.

## Beginner

Can immediately understand:

- where to start
- what is required
- how to run an analysis

without seeing hundreds of options.

---

## Power User

Can expose nearly every parameter.

Should never feel constrained.

Advanced controls should exist without overwhelming beginners.

---

## Developer

Can inspect:

- artifacts
- collector
- QA
- metadata
- render targets
- diagnostics

without cluttering the normal workflow.

---

# Progressive Disclosure

Every screen should expose only what is immediately necessary.

Suggested hierarchy:

Required

â†“

Common Options

â†“

Advanced Options

â†“

Artifact Settings

â†“

Developer Tools

â†“

QA

Do not expose every available parameter immediately simply because it exists.

---

# Workflow-Centric Design

The application should guide users through an analytical workflow rather than presenting disconnected modules.

Conceptually:

Project

â†“

Data

â†“

Analysis

â†“

Artifacts

â†“

Reports

â†“

Collector

â†“

AI Ready

Every page should reinforce this workflow.

---

# Project Workspace

Design a unified Project Workspace.

Examples of information to surface:

Project Name

Current Dataset

Completed Modules

Pending Modules

Collector Status

Artifact Counts

Quality Score

QA Status

Render Targets

Recent Activity

Generated Reports

Generated ... [truncated]

.aq_class <- function(...) {
  classes <- unlist(list(...), use.names = FALSE)
  classes <- classes[!is.null(classes) & nzchar(classes)]
  paste(classes, collapse = " ")
}

ui_card <- function(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, ..., footer = NULL, class = NULL) {
  tags$section(
    class = .aq_class("aq-card", class),
    if (!is.null(title) || !is.null(subtitle)) {
      tags$header(
        class = "aq-card-header",
        if (!is.null(title)) tags$h3(class = "aq-card-title", title),
        if (!is.null(subtitle)) tags$p(class = "aq-card-subtitle", subtitle)
      )
    },
    tags$div(class = "aq-card-body", ...),
    if (!is.null(footer)) tags$footer(class = "aq-card-footer", footer)
  )
}

ui_app_shell <- function(..., theme = "light") {
  theme <- match.arg(theme, c("light", "dark", "pimp"))

  tagList(
    tags$script(HTML(sprintf(
      "document.body.classList.add('aq-theme-%s');",
      theme
    ))),
    tags$div(class = "aq-app-shell", ...)
  )
}

ui_page <- function(title, subtitle = NULL, ...) {
  tags$div(
    class = "aq-page",
    ui_section_header(title, subtitle),
    ...
  )
}

ui_section_header <- function(title, subtitle = NULL) {
  tags$header(
    class = "aq-section-header",
    tags$h2(class = "aq-section-title", title),
    if (!is.null(subtitle)) tags$p(class = "aq-section-subtitle", subtitle)
  )
}

ui_empty_state <- function(title, message = NULL, icon = NULL) {
  tags$div(
    class = "aq-empty-state",
    if (!is.null(icon)) tags$div(class = "aq-empty-state-icon", icon),
    tags$h3(class = "aq-empty-state-title", title),
    if (!is.null(message)) tags$p(class = "aq-empty-state-message", message)
  )
}

ui_status_badge <- function(
  label,
  status = c("success", "warning", "error", "info", "neutral")
) {
  status <- match.arg(status)

  tags$span(
    class = .aq_class("aq-status-badge", paste0("aq-status-badge-", status)),
    label
  )
}

ui_action_row <- function(...) {
  tags$div(class = "aq-action-row", ...)
}

ui_control_group <- function(title = NULL, ..., description = NULL) {
  tags$section(
    class = "aq-control-group",
    if (!is.null(title)) tags$h3(class = "aq-control-group-title", title),
    if (!is.null(description)) tags$p(class = "aq-control-group-description", description),
    tags$div(class = "aq-control-group-body", ...)
  )
}

  style <- if (!is.null(height)) paste0("min-h ... [truncated]

## 272. 2026-07-08T06:06:30.915Z - current_projectless_megathread - user

# IMPORTANT ARCHITECTURAL UPDATE

Before continuing the current UX/UI implementation, update the design approach with the following philosophy.

The goal is NOT to build "a nice Shiny application."

The goal is to build a premium analytics workstation that happens to use Shiny as its reactive engine.

Shiny should NOT define the UX.

Shiny should provide:

- reactivity
- state management
- module orchestration
- server communication
- routing

Everything else should be considered replaceable if a better implementation exists.

---

# Revised UX Philosophy

Do NOT limit yourself to stock Shiny components.

Do NOT assume existing Shiny widgets are the best user experience.

If a custom HTML/CSS/JavaScript component would provide a substantially better analytical experience, prefer the custom implementation.

The world is our oyster.

Use modern JavaScript libraries where appropriate and expose them cleanly through reusable R/Shiny wrappers.

---

# Build an Internal Design System

Rather than styling existing widgets individually, create reusable UI primitives.

Examples include:

Cards

Metric Tiles

Status Badges

Progress Indicators

Artifact Preview Cards

Collector Status Panels

Timeline Components

Workflow Progress Components

Section Headers

Information Callouts

Warning Panels

Success Panels

Empty States

Loading States

Notification Toasts

Action Bars

Split Panels

Resizable Panels

Tabbed Workspaces

Dockable Side Panels

Search Panels

Command Palette

Artifact Gallery

Project Dashboard

These should become reusable building blocks throughout the application.

---

# Shiny Responsibility

Use Shiny only for:

- state
- inputs
- outputs
- modules
- reactivity

Do not force the UX to resemble traditional Shiny applications.

---

# Modern Interaction Patterns

Prefer interaction models inspired by modern professional software.

Examples include:

VS Code

Cursor

Figma

Linear

Notion

JetBrains IDEs

Adobe Lightroom

Power BI Desktop

The application should feel like professional analytical software rather than a traditional dashboard.

---

# Workspace Philosophy

The application should feel like an analytical workspace.

Not:

Page

↓

Run

↓

Output

Instead:

Project

↓

Workspace

↓

Analysis

↓

Artifacts

↓

Collector

↓

Reports

↓

AI

Everything should reinforce this workflow.

---

# Progressive Disclosure

Maintain the existing API philosophy.

The UI should expose:

Simple

↓

Common

↓

Advanced

↓

Developer

↓

QA

Power sho ... [truncated]

## 284. 2026-07-08T06:11:30.093Z - current_projectless_megathread - assistant

Implemented the revised UX/UI direction as a workstation design-system pass, not a stock Shiny refresh.

Changed:
- [R/ui_components.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/ui_components.R:1)
  - Added reusable workstation primitives: action bars, split panels, stat tiles, callouts, progress steps, artifact preview cards, collector status panels, activity lists, disclosure panels.
  - Added `qa_ui_consistency()`.
- [www/app.css](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/www/app.css:1)
  - Added styling for the internal design system and denser workstation layout patterns.
- [R/page_project.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/page_project.R:1)
  - Reworked Project into a Project Workspace with status tiles, recent activity, artifact/report/collector visibility, render target visibility, and progressive bundle options.
- [R/app_server.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/app_server.R:229)
  - Collector summary now exposes `render_target`.
- [R/page_workflow.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/page_workflow.R:340)
  - Workflow collector summary now shows render target.
- [R/app_ui.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/app_ui.R:1)
  - Shell title now reads `Analytics Workstation`.
- [docs/ui_ux_architecture.md](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/docs/ui_ux_architecture.md:1)
  - Documents the updated philosophy: Shiny as reactive engine, not UX ceiling; internal design system; custom component opportunities.

Validation:
- `qa_ui_consistency()`: 12/12 passed
- `qa_analysis_modules_integration()`: 14 success, 1 existing warning, 0 errors
- `git diff --check`: clean

I did not rebuild every screen. This establishes the reusable workstation layer and upgrades the highest-impact surface first: the Project Workspace.

# Task: Premium Dark-First UX/UI Workstation Pass

## Background

The Analytics Workstation architecture is now mature.

Implemented:

- Artifact Model
- Project Artifact Collector
- Render Targets
- Artifact Quality Policy
- Table Artifact Architecture
- Producer Semantics
- Workstation Design System
- Project Workspace
- UI Component Library
- Comprehensive QA

The next phase is no longer backend architecture.

The next phase is creating a premium analytical experience.

This is NOT a CSS refresh.

This is NOT a Shiny facelift.

The goal is to build an analytical workstation whose visual quality equals or exceeds:

- AutoPlots
- Generated RMarkdown reports
- Modern professional analytical software

Shiny should remain the reactive engine, not the UX ceiling.

---

# Primary Objective

Transform AnalyticsShinyApp into a premium dark-first analytical workstation.

The application should feel like purpose-built professional software rather than a traditional Shiny application.

The experience should be:

- beautiful
- ergonomic
- information dense
- consistent
- enjoyable
- extremely efficient

---

# Visual Philosophy

The app should become the flagship visual experience.

Today:

AutoPlots
â†“

beautiful visualizations

RMarkdown
â†“

beautiful analytical stories

AnalyticsShinyApp
â†“

should become the beautiful analytical workspace that creates both.

The application should visually outshine both while remaining part of the same product family.

---

# Design Language

Treat AutoPlots and the generated RMarkdown reports as the existing visual DNA.

Do not simply restyle the application independently.

Instead:

Audit:

- AutoPlots default dark theme
- RMarkdown CSS
- Existing AnalyticsShinyApp CSS

Extract the shared visual language.

Examples:

background colors

surface colors

panel colors

accent colors

typography

table styling

card styling

spacing

margins

padding

borders

corner radius

shadows

hover behavior

status colors

warning colors

success colors

error colors

loading colors

focus states

Create a shared dark design language.

The application should become the premium evolution of that language.

---

# Shared Design Tokens

Create or improve a centralized design token system.

Avoid hard-coded styling throughout the application.

Examples:

background

surface

surface elevated

primary

secondary

success

warning

danger

text primary

text secondary

muted text

border ... [truncated]

.aq_class <- function(...) {
  classes <- unlist(list(...), use.names = FALSE)
  classes <- classes[!is.null(classes) & nzchar(classes)]
  paste(classes, collapse = " ")
}

ui_card <- function(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, ..., footer = NULL, class = NULL) {
  tags$section(
    class = .aq_class("aq-card", class),
    if (!is.null(title) || !is.null(subtitle)) {
      tags$header(
        class = "aq-card-header",
        if (!is.null(title)) tags$h3(class = "aq-card-title", title),
        if (!is.null(subtitle)) tags$p(class = "aq-card-subtitle", subtitle)
      )
    },
    tags$div(class = "aq-card-body", ...),
    if (!is.null(footer)) tags$footer(class = "aq-card-footer", footer)
  )
}

ui_app_shell <- function(..., theme = "light") {
  theme <- match.arg(theme, c("light", "dark", "pimp"))

  tagList(
    tags$script(HTML(sprintf(
      "document.body.classList.add('aq-theme-%s');",
      theme
    ))),
    tags$div(class = "aq-app-shell", ...)
  )
}

ui_page <- function(title, subtitle = NULL, ..., actions = NULL, eyebrow = NULL) {
  tags$div(
    class = "aq-page",
    ui_section_header(title, subtitle, actions = actions, eyebrow = eyebrow),
    ...
  )
}

ui_section_header <- function(title, subtitle = NULL, actions = NULL, eyebrow = NULL) {
  tags$header(
    class = "aq-section-header",
    tags$div(
      class = "aq-section-heading",
      if (!is.null(eyebrow)) tags$p(class = "aq-section-eyebrow", eyebrow),
      tags$h2(class = "aq-section-title", title),
      if (!is.null(subtitle)) tags$p(class = "aq-section-subtitle", subtitle)
    ),
    if (!is.null(actions)) tags$div(class = "aq-section-actions", actions)
  )
}

ui_empty_state <- function(title, message = NULL, icon = NULL) {
  tags$div(
    class = "aq-empty-state",
    if (!is.null(icon)) tags$div(class = "aq-empty-state-icon", icon),
    tags$h3(class = "aq-empty-state-title", title),
    if (!is.null(message)) tags$p(class = "aq-empty-state-message", message)
  )
}

ui_status_badge <- function(
  label,
  status = c("success", "warning", "error", "info", "neutral")
) {
  status <- match.arg(status)

  tags$span(
    class = .aq_class("aq-status-badge", paste0("aq-status-badge-", status)),
    label
  )
}

ui_action_row <- function(...) {
  tags$div(class = "aq-action-row", ...)
}

ui_action_bar <- function(..., left = NULL, right = NULL) {
  tags$div(
    class = "aq-action-bar",
    tags$div(class = "aq-action-bar-left", l ... [truncated]

page_project_ui <- function(id) {
  ns <- NS(id)

  tabPanel(
    "Project",
    ui_page(
      title = "Project Workspace",
      subtitle = "Mission control for data, modules, artifacts, reports, collector output, and AI-ready evidence.",
      eyebrow = "Project",
      actions = ui_action_row(
        actionButton(ns("save_project"), "Save Project", class = "btn-primary"),
        actionButton(ns("load_project"), "Load Project", class = "btn-secondary")
      ),
      uiOutput(ns("workspace_overview")),
      ui_workspace_grid(
        columns = "main-sidebar",
        tagList(
          ui_card(
            title = "Workspace Status",
            subtitle = "A compact readout of the current analytical run.",
            uiOutput(ns("project_message_panel")),
            uiOutput(ns("workspace_status")),
            ui_disclosure(
              "Recent Activity",
              uiOutput(ns("recent_activity")),
              level = "common",
              open = TRUE
            )
          ),
          uiOutput(ns("collector_panel"))
        ),
        ui_card(
          title = "Project Files",
          subtitle = "Save or reload the project state and portable bundle.",
          textInput(
            ns("project_path"),
            "Project File",
            value = file.path(getwd(), "autoplots_project.rds")
          ),
          ui_action_row(
            actionButton(ns("save_project_secondary"), "Save", class = "btn-primary btn-sm"),
            actionButton(ns("load_project_secondary"), "Load", class = "btn-secondary btn-sm")
          ),
          ui_disclosure(
            "Bundle Options",
            textInput(
              ns("bundle_dir"),
              "Project Bundle Directory",
              value = file.path(getwd(), "autoplots_project")
            ),
            ui_action_row(
              actionButton(ns("save_bundle"), "Save Bundle", class = "btn-primary btn-sm"),
              actionButton(ns("load_bundle"), "Load Bundle", class = "btn-secondary btn-sm")
            ),
            level = "advanced"
          )
        )
      )
    )
  )
}

# AnalyticsShinyApp UX/UI Architecture

## Design Philosophy

AnalyticsShinyApp should feel like a premium analytical workstation: compact, intentional, artifact-aware, and workflow-centered. The app should guide beginners without hiding power from advanced users or developers.

The product goal is not to build a nice Shiny application. The goal is to build professional analytical software that happens to use Shiny as its reactive engine.

Shiny provides:

- reactivity
- state management
- module orchestration
- server communication
- routing

Shiny does not define the UX language. Components, layouts, interaction models, and visual hierarchy are owned by the AnalyticsShinyApp design system and can use custom HTML/CSS/JavaScript when that produces a better analytical experience.

The product principle is:

```text
Simple by default
Powerful when expanded
Inspectable when needed
```

The design inspiration should be closer to professional workspaces such as VS Code, Cursor, Figma, Linear, JetBrains IDEs, Adobe Lightroom, and Power BI Desktop than to a traditional dashboard.

## Workflow Philosophy

The app is organized around the analytical lifecycle:

```text
Project -> Data -> Analysis -> Artifacts -> Reports -> Collector -> AI Ready
```

Every page should answer:

- Where am I?
- What can I do?
- What should I do next?

The Project Workspace is the home surface for overall status. Workflow remains the lifecycle launchpad. Analysis Modules remain the parameterized execution surface.

## Layout Principles

- Use `ui_page()` for page shells.
- Use `ui_card()` for bounded task surfaces.
- Use `ui_action_bar()` for persistent local action zones.
- Use `ui_workspace_grid()` for main/sidebar or multi-column workstation layouts.
- Use `ui_split_panel()` for analytical work areas with an inspector/sidebar.
- Use `ui_stat_grid()` and `ui_stat_tile()` for project status, artifact counts, collector state, and QA summaries.
- Use `ui_action_row()` for action placement.
- Use `ui_callout()` for contextual information, warnings, and success states.
- Use `ui_progress_steps()` for workflow/stage progress.
- Use `ui_artifact_preview_card()` for artifact gallery and result surfaces.
- Use `ui_collector_status_panel()` anywhere collector state needs to be visible.
- Use `ui_empty_state()` whenever a panel has no content.
- Keep dense operational pages compact; avoid landing-page composition inside the app.

Stock Shi ... [truncated]

## 323. 2026-07-08T06:35:21.357Z - current_projectless_megathread - user

# Task: Fix Dark Theme Control and Table Styling

The premium dark workstation pass improved the shell, but visual QA shows two major regressions:

1. Tables are rendering as default/light tables.
2. Select inputs/dropdowns/text inputs are rendering as default/light Shiny controls.

This breaks the dark-first workstation experience.

## Objective

Create dark-first reusable styling for all form controls and tables.

## Scope

Fix at minimum:

- selectInput / selectizeInput
- textInput
- numericInput
- checkboxInput
- radioButtons
- actionButton
- downloadButton
- reactable tables
- plain HTML tables
- DT/DataTables if present
- tableOutput / renderTable outputs

## Requirements

- Use the existing dark token system in `www/app.css`.
- Do not create one-off page-specific CSS.
- Prefer reusable component classes.
- Match or improve the existing custom Rmd/reactable dark table styling.
- Tables should no longer render with white backgrounds.
- Inputs should no longer render as default white browser/Shiny controls.
- Dropdown menus/options should be dark styled where technically possible.
- Preserve usability, contrast, hover states, focus states, disabled states.
- Do not change backend logic.
- Do not regress report generation.

## Reactable

Audit current Rmd custom reactable styling and port the best parts into the app design system.

Create a shared dark reactable style helper if appropriate.

## QA

Expand `qa_ui_consistency()` to detect:

- default/light table classes in app pages
- unstyled `tableOutput`/HTML tables
- unstyled select/text/numeric controls where detectable
- missing workstation table/control classes

## Visual Acceptance

After the change:

- Workflow summary table should look dark and workstation-native.
- Project Artifact Collector table should look dark and workstation-native.
- Analysis Module selectors should look dark and polished.
- Input fields should visually belong to the app.
- No major white control blocks should appear in the dark UI.

## Documentation

Update `docs/ui_ux_architecture.md` with:

- dark control styling rules
- dark table styling rules
- reactable styling guidance
- preferred table/control components


## 324. 2026-07-08T06:35:32.805Z - current_projectless_megathread - assistant

.table_as_data_table <- function(data) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(data.table::data.table())
  }

  data.table::as.data.table(data)
}

.format_table_numbers <- function(data, digits = 3) {
  data <- data.table::copy(.table_as_data_table(data))
  numeric_cols <- names(data)[vapply(data, is.numeric, logical(1))]
  for (column in numeric_cols) {
    data[[column]] <- round(data[[column]], digits = digits)
  }

  data
}

aq_reactable_exclusion_filter <- function() {
  if (!requireNamespace("htmlwidgets", quietly = TRUE)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  htmlwidgets::JS("
    function(rows, columnId, filterValue) {
      if (!filterValue) return rows;

      const filter = String(filterValue).toLowerCase().trim();
      const exclude = filter.startsWith('!') || filter.startsWith('-');
      const term = exclude ? filter.slice(1).trim() : filter;

      if (!term) return rows;

      return rows.filter(function(row) {
        const rawValue = row.values[columnId];
        const value = rawValue == null ? '' : String(rawValue).toLowerCase();
        const match = value.includes(term);
        return exclude ? !match : match;
      });
    }
  ")
}

aq_reactable_is_text_like <- function(x) {
  is.character(x) || is.factor(x) || is.logical(x)
}

aq_reactable_column_defs <- function(data, digits = 3) {
  data <- .table_as_data_table(data)
  numeric_cols <- names(data)[vapply(data, is.numeric, logical(1))]
  text_cols <- names(data)[vapply(data, aq_reactable_is_text_like, logical(1))]
  exclusion_filter <- aq_reactable_exclusion_filter()

  stats::setNames(
    lapply(names(data), function(column) {
      if (column %in% numeric_cols) {
        return(reactable::colDef(format = reactable::colFormat(digits = digits)))
      }

      if (column %in% text_cols && !is.null(exclusion_filter)) {
        return(reactable::colDef(filterMethod = exclusion_filter))
      }

      reactable::colDef()
    }),
    names(data)
  )
}

render_table <- function(
  data,
  engine = c("reactable", "html"),
  title = NULL,
  subtitle = NULL,
  page_size = 10,
  searchable = TRUE,
  sortable = TRUE,
  filterable = FALSE,
  digits = 3,
  theme = c("auto", "light", "dark", "pimp")
) {
  engine <- match.arg(engine)
  theme <- .normalize_table_theme(theme)

  table <- if (identical(engine, "reactable") &&
               requireNamespace("reactable", quietly = TRUE)) {
    render_reactable_table(
      data = data,
      page_size = pa ... [truncated]

.normalize_table_theme <- function(theme = c("auto", "light", "dark", "pimp")) {
  theme <- match.arg(theme)
  if (identical(theme, "auto")) {
    return(getOption("aq.theme", "light"))
  }

  theme
}

get_reactable_theme <- function(theme = c("auto", "light", "dark", "pimp")) {
  theme <- .normalize_table_theme(theme)
  if (!requireNamespace("reactable", quietly = TRUE)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  switch(
    theme,
    dark = reactable_theme_dark(),
    pimp = reactable_theme_pimp(),
    reactable_theme_light()
  )
}

reactable_theme_light <- function() {
  reactable::reactableTheme(
    color = "#334155",
    backgroundColor = "#ffffff",
    borderColor = "#d8dee8",
    stripedColor = "#f8fafc",
    highlightColor = "#eef4ff",
    cellPadding = "8px 10px",
    headerStyle = list(
      background = "#f8fafc",
      color = "#334155",
      borderColor = "#d8dee8",
      fontWeight = "600"
    ),
    searchInputStyle = list(
      background = "#ffffff",
      color = "#111827",
      borderColor = "#cbd5e1"
    )
  )
}

reactable_theme_dark <- function() {
  reactable::reactableTheme(
    color = "#e5e7eb",
    backgroundColor = "#111827",
    borderColor = "#334155",
    stripedColor = "#1f2937",
    highlightColor = "#1e3a5f",
    cellPadding = "8px 10px",
    headerStyle = list(
      background = "#1f2937",
      color = "#f8fafc",
      borderColor = "#334155",
      fontWeight = "600"
    ),
    searchInputStyle = list(
      background = "#0b1220",
      color = "#f8fafc",
      borderColor = "#475569"
    )
  )
}

reactable_theme_pimp <- function() {
  reactable::reactableTheme(
    color = "#f8fafc",
    backgroundColor = "#0f172a",
    borderColor = "#7c3aed",
    stripedColor = "#1e293b",
    highlightColor = "#164e63",
    cellPadding = "8px 10px",
    headerStyle = list(
      background = "#32105f",
      color = "#a5f3fc",
      borderColor = "#22d3ee",
      fontWeight = "700"
    ),
    searchInputStyle = list(
      background = "#f8fafc",
      color = "#111827",
      borderColor = "#67e8f9"
    )
  )
}

Note: I'm going to bed so do a very long and very exhaustive search so that we are well equipped in the morning to dominate this UI / UX realm. I want to be frontier.

# Task: UI/UX Evolution and Innovation Research Sprint

Do not implement code yet.

Study the historical evolution of software UI patterns across multiple categories, not just ML tools.

Research and summarize patterns from:

- command line / terminal workflows
- desktop IDEs
- creative tools
- trading terminals
- BI tools
- notebook environments
- analytics dashboards
- design tools
- AI-native tools
- control-room / mission-control interfaces
- report/storytelling interfaces

Examples to consider:

- Bloomberg Terminal
- VS Code
- Cursor
- JetBrains IDEs
- Figma
- Linear
- Notion
- Power BI Desktop
- Tableau
- JupyterLab
- Observable
- Databricks
- Adobe Lightroom
- NASA / industrial control-room dashboards
- modern AI agent workspaces

Research:

1. Historical evolution
   - CLI
   - GUI
   - skeuomorphism
   - flat design
   - material design
   - dark-first professional tools
   - AI-agent interfaces
   - spatial / interactive workspaces

2. Layout patterns
   - command palette
   - side rail
   - dockable panels
   - split panes
   - inspector panels
   - artifact galleries
   - timelines
   - card walls
   - canvas workspaces
   - workflow graphs
   - activity feeds
   - status centers

3. Interaction patterns
   - progressive disclosure
   - keyboard-first workflows
   - drag/drop
   - resizable panels
   - hover previews
   - live search
   - agent plan/execute
   - confidence/status signaling
   - preview-before-commit

4. Signature “wow” moments
   - what makes the software memorable?
   - what creates delight without harming productivity?
   - what patterns could translate into Analytics Workstation?

5. Output
   Produce:
   - a research summary
   - a pattern library
   - 20–30 candidate UI ideas
   - 5 signature “holy shit” moments for Analytics Workstation
   - 3 radically different workspace concepts with ASCII wireframes

Do not constrain ideas to stock Shiny components.

Assume custom HTML/CSS/JS components are allowed.

The goal is to identify the best interaction and layout ideas available today, then evolve them into something better for end-to-end analytics.

# Task: Integrate the UI/UX Research Sprint into the Repository and Convert It into an Actionable Product Roadmap

The recent UI/UX Research Sprint produced one of the most important design artifacts for Analytics Workstation.

It currently exists outside the repository.

That is not desirable.

The research should become part of the project's long-term design documentation.

However, do not simply copy the document into the repo.

Treat it as a living design reference.

---

## Objectives

1. Move the research into the repository.

2. Organize it appropriately.

3. Separate timeless product vision from implementation roadmap.

4. Identify actionable UX milestones.

---

## Repository Organization

Create (or update if appropriate) a structure similar to:

docs/

    architecture/

    vision/

    research/

or another organization that better fits the existing documentation.

The UI/UX research should live under Research.

The long-term philosophy should live under Vision.

---

## Produce Three Documents

### 1. Product Vision

This should be concise.

Capture ideas such as:

- Analytics Workstation is an analytical operating environment.
- The project is the world.
- Modules are producers.
- Artifacts are evidence.
- The collector is memory.
- AI reasons over evidence.
- Human reports and LLM artifacts are different render targets.
- The workstation exists to create understanding rather than dashboards.

This document should remain relatively stable.

---

### 2. UI/UX Research

Move the complete research into the repository.

Preserve:

historical evolution

software comparisons

pattern library

workspace concepts

holy-shit ideas

interaction patterns

AI patterns

Do not discard ideas.

This becomes a long-term research reference.

---

### 3. UX Roadmap

Extract implementation work.

Organize into phases.

Examples:

Phase 1

Shell

Command palette

Artifact Studio

Mission Control

Phase 2

Artifact filmstrip

Inspector

Workflow graph

Project Health

Phase 3

Agentic Lab

Model Landscape

Spatial workspace

Evidence bundles

Each milestone should include:

Purpose

Dependencies

Expected UX benefit

Estimated complexity

Priority

This roadmap should evolve over time.

---

## Reframe One Important Concept

The research currently discusses:

Mission Control

Artifact Studio

Agentic Lab

These should no longer be described primarily as pages.

Instead describe them as:

Workstation Modes

Similar to Lightroom modules or IDE workspaces.

# UI/UX Evolution and Innovation Research Sprint

Date: 2026-07-08

Scope: research only. No implementation recommendations here assume stock Shiny components. Custom HTML/CSS/JavaScript, Shiny wrappers, local state, keyboard systems, browser APIs, and future AI-agent orchestration are all considered available.

## Executive Thesis

AnalyticsShinyApp should not become a prettier dashboard. The frontier move is to become an analytical operating environment: part IDE, part Bloomberg Terminal, part Lightroom, part Tableau, part JupyterLab, part AI-agent cockpit.

The best tools in history converge on the same pattern:

```text
fast command surface
+ persistent project context
+ flexible workbench layout
+ inspectable artifacts
+ visible system state
+ reversible operations
+ progressive power
+ strong defaults
+ AI that plans, explains, and executes with evidence
```

For Analytics Workstation, the most important product bet is this:

```text
The project is the world.
Modules are producers.
Artifacts are evidence.
The collector is memory.
The AI is a copilot/analyst who navigates evidence, not a chat box bolted on the side.
```

## Source Map

Primary and reference sources used:

- GUI evolution: GUI history and transition from command line to desktop metaphor, skeuomorphism, flat design, and material design [The Evolution of the Graphical User Interface](https://ejournals.epublishing.ekt.gr/index.php/DAC/article/view/27466)
- CLI history and shell workflows: CLI origins, command history, aliases, scripting, and automation [Contentstack CLI history](https://www.contentstack.com/blog/tech-talk/the-evolution-of-command-line-interface-cli-a-historical-insight)
- Bloomberg Terminal: command line plus mnemonics, help key, integrated market data, analytics, communication, and AI transition [Bloomberg Terminal](https://professional.bloomberg.com/products/bloomberg-terminal/), [Bloomberg guide PDF](https://blogs.kent.ac.uk/kbs-news-events/files/2017/10/Bloomberg-Terminal-Guide.pdf), [Wired ASKB coverage](https://www.wired.com/story/the-bloomberg-terminal-is-getting-an-ai-makeover-like-it-or-not)
- VS Code: editor groups, primary/secondary sidebars, panel, status bar, customizable layout [VS Code UI docs](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editing/userinterface), [VS Code custom layout](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/configure/custom-layout), [VS Code sidebar guidelines](https://code.visualstudio.com/api/ux- ... [truncated]

# AnalyticsShinyApp UX/UI Architecture

## Design Philosophy

AnalyticsShinyApp should feel like a premium analytical workstation: compact, intentional, artifact-aware, and workflow-centered. The app should guide beginners without hiding power from advanced users or developers.

The product goal is not to build a nice Shiny application. The goal is to build professional analytical software that happens to use Shiny as its reactive engine.

Shiny provides:

- reactivity
- state management
- module orchestration
- server communication
- routing

Shiny does not define the UX language. Components, layouts, interaction models, and visual hierarchy are owned by the AnalyticsShinyApp design system and can use custom HTML/CSS/JavaScript when that produces a better analytical experience.

The product principle is:

```text
Simple by default
Powerful when expanded
Inspectable when needed
```

The design inspiration should be closer to professional workspaces such as VS Code, Cursor, Figma, Linear, JetBrains IDEs, Adobe Lightroom, and Power BI Desktop than to a traditional dashboard.

## Visual Language

AnalyticsShinyApp is dark-first. This matches the production AutoPlots and AutoQuant artifact/report defaults, where dark themes are the normal analytical rendering path. Light and pimp modes remain available as explicit alternatives, but the app shell, core tokens, artifact previews, and report-oriented surfaces should treat dark mode as the primary design target.

The shared palette uses:

- deep navy base surfaces for the workstation shell
- elevated blue-navy panels for task surfaces
- blue and cyan accents for focus, active navigation, and analytical action
- green, amber, and red for success, warning, and failure states
- compact 8px radii and dense spacing for operational software rather than marketing composition

Color, spacing, radius, focus, and shadow decisions belong in `www/app.css` tokens. Pages should not define one-off palettes.

## Control Styling

All Shiny controls must visually belong to the workstation shell. Pages may use normal Shiny inputs for behavior, but their visual treatment should come from shared CSS selectors and tokens rather than page-specific overrides.

Dark-first control rules:

- `selectInput()` and `selectizeInput()` must use dark input backgrounds, dark dropdown menus, visible active/selected states, and tokenized focus rings.
- `textInput()`, `numericInput()`, `textArea ... [truncated]

  - Concise long-term identity: Analytics Workstation as an evidence-centered analytical operating environment.

  - Reframes Mission Control, Artifact Studio, and Agentic Lab as Workstation Modes.

- [docs/research/ui_ux_research_sprint.md](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/docs/research/ui_ux_research_sprint.md)

## 354. 2026-07-08T14:58:37.595Z - current_projectless_megathread - user

# Task: Build Artifact Studio (Phase 1)

The Product Vision, Research, and UX Roadmap are now established.

Do not work on other workstation modes.

Do not redesign the shell.

Do not modify backend architecture.

Focus exclusively on implementing the first version of Artifact Studio.

## Objective

Create the place where users naturally spend most of their analytical time.

Artifacts become the center of the experience.

The Artifact Studio should immediately feel different from a traditional Shiny application.

---

## Scope

Build only the Artifact Studio.

Use the existing:

- Artifact Model
- Project Artifact Collector
- Artifact Quality Policy
- Table Artifact Architecture
- Producer Semantics
- Workstation Design System

Do not duplicate existing functionality.

---

## Initial Layout

Artifact Studio should contain four coordinated regions.

LEFT

Artifact filters

Project collections

Artifact types

Runs

Modules

Quality

CENTER

Artifact gallery

Cards

Preview thumbnails

Quick metadata

RIGHT

Artifact Inspector

Large preview

Caption

Diagnostics

Recommendations

Metadata

Collector information

Backing assets

BOTTOM

Persistent artifact filmstrip

Recently generated artifacts

Quick switching

---

## Artifact Cards

Each artifact should become a visual object.

Examples:

Plot thumbnail

Table icon

Narrative icon

Recommendation icon

JSON icon

Collector badge

Each card should show:

Title

Module

Run

Quality

Importance

Analytical intent

Render targets

Hover actions

Open

Inspect

Compare

Add to Story (placeholder)

---

## Artifact Inspector

Selecting an artifact should populate the inspector.

Examples:

Large preview

Caption

Purpose

Quality score

Diagnostics

Recommendations

Metadata

CSV available

JSON available

Collector location

Render targets

Producer

Do not implement editing yet.

Inspection only.

---

## Filmstrip

Create a persistent artifact filmstrip.

Recently generated artifacts should appear automatically.

The filmstrip should support:

hover

select

scroll

quick navigation

The filmstrip becomes a reusable workstation component.

---

## Empty State

When no artifacts exist:

Do not leave the page blank.

Instead guide the user toward generating analyses.

---

## UX Goal

Artifact Studio should feel like:

the Lightroom Library module

meets

an analytical evidence browser.

Users should immediately want to click things.

Exploration should feel natural.

---

## QA

Add:

qa_artifact_studio() ... [truncated]

## 356. 2026-07-08T14:58:54.639Z - current_projectless_megathread - assistant

The existing Artifact Library already has the right data source, but it is still mostly a management table plus preview. I’m going to replace that surface with an Artifact Studio UI while keeping the same underlying artifact state and table export actions intact.


## 357. 2026-07-08T14:58:58.983Z - current_projectless_megathread - assistant

I’m going to add Artifact Studio-specific UI components to the reusable component layer instead of hardcoding card/filmstrip/inspector markup in the page. That gives us the first reusable “wow” surface without duplicating backend artifact logic.


## 358. 2026-07-08T14:59:15.529Z - current_projectless_megathread - assistant

The artifact model already carries source module, section, visibility/status, producer semantics, quality scoring, captions, and table sidecars. ## 365. 2026-07-08T15:04:23.138Z - current_projectless_megathread - assistant

Implemented Artifact Studio Phase 1.

What changed:
- Replaced the old Artifact Library surface with **Artifact Studio** in [R/page_artifact_library.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/page_artifact_library.R).
- Added the four coordinated regions:
  - left filters and project collections
  - center artifact gallery
  - right artifact inspector
  - bottom persistent artifact filmstrip
- Added reusable workstation components in [R/ui_components.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/ui_components.R):
  - `ui_artifact_studio_card()`
  - `ui_artifact_filmstrip()`
  - artifact type icon/safe-id helpers
- Added Artifact Studio CSS in [www/app.css](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/www/app.css).
- Added `qa_artifact_studio()` and wired it into aggregate QA in [R/module_result.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/module_result.R).

Behavior:
- Artifact cards now show title, module, run, quality, importance, intent, render targets, and hover actions.
- Inspector shows preview, caption/purpose, quality, diagnostics, recommendations, metadata, collector info, and backing asset status.
- Filmstrip supports quick artifact selection.
- Empty states guide users toward generating artifacts.
- Compare and Add to Story are explicit placeholders only, per scope.

Validation:
- `qa_artifact_studio()`: 12/12 success.
- `qa_ui_consistency()`: 28/28 success.
- `qa_analysis_modules_integration()`: 15 success, 1 existing warning, 0 errors.
- `git diff --check`: clean, only Git LF/CRLF warnings.
- App restarted and responding at [http://127.0.0.1:3899](http://127.0.0.1:3899).

- card layout
- thumbnail/preview treatment
- inspector hierarchy
- filmstrip usefulness
- spacing/density
- hover states
- empty state
- metadata overload
- whether the page feels exciting or still form-like

Produce a prioritized punch list.

Then implement only low-risk, high-impact polish fixes.

Do not implement Compare, Story Builder, Mission Control, or Agentic Lab yet.

Acceptance criteria:

- Artifact Studio feels visually distinct from a normal Shiny page.
- Artifacts feel like first-class objects.
- The inspector is useful without being cluttered.
- The filmstrip adds value rather than decoration.
- QA still passes.

## 385. 2026-07-08T15:20:29.971Z - current_projectless_megathread - assistant

Implemented the low-risk Artifact Studio polish pass and verified it in the live app.

**Prioritized Punch List**
1. **Filmstrip was too decorative / too large**: fixed. It is now a compact persistent evidence lane instead of a bottom panel that steals the page.
2. **Empty state felt too plain/form-like**: fixed. The gallery now gives directional “Generate evidence / Create manual artifacts / Return here” cards.
3. **Inspector empty state needed hierarchy**: fixed. It now explains what will appear there and shows compact placeholder areas for preview, quality, diagnostics, and backing assets.
4. **Left filter rail was cramped/overflow-prone**: fixed. Inputs are constrained and compacted inside the rail.
5. **Gallery needed denser, more object-like treatment**: improved. Empty-state cards now feel more like artifact entry points.
6. **Remaining gap**: populated-artifact visual QA still needs a pass once there are real screenshots/tables/artifacts in the Studio. Current pass validated the empty and structural state.

**Files Changed**
- [R/page_artifact_library.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/page_artifact_library.R)
- [R/ui_components.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/ui_components.R)
- [www/app.css](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/www/app.css)

**Validation**
- `qa_artifact_studio()`: 12 success, 0 failures
- `qa_ui_consistency()`: 28 success, 0 failures
- `qa_analysis_modules_integration()`: 15 success, 1 existing warning, 0 errors
- `git diff --check`: clean, only existing LF/CRLF warnings
- Live app verified at `http://127.0.0.1:3899`

I did not add Compare, Story Builder, Mission Control, Agentic Lab, or new backend architecture.

## My request for Codex:
# Task: Create Artifact Studio Demo Seed

Generate a reproducible demo project/run that produces real artifacts for Artifact Studio visual QA.

Use synthetic data.

Run at least:
- EDA
- Model Readiness
- Model Insights or SHAP if available
- Collector append

Goal:
Artifact Studio should populate with real plot thumbnails, table artifacts, narratives, diagnostics, recommendations, and collector metadata.

Do not redesign Artifact Studio.

Do not add new architecture.

Create a repeatable helper such as:
create_artifact_studio_demo_project()

QA should verify:
- artifacts exist
- gallery has populated cards
- inspector can display real artifact metadata
- filmstrip has real entries
- collector has real bundles
- screenshots/tables/sidecars exist where applicable

Then run visual QA on the populated Artifact Studio and produce a prioritized punch list.

page_project_ui <- function(id) {
  ns <- NS(id)

  tabPanel(
    "Project",
    ui_page(
      title = "Project Workspace",
      subtitle = "Mission control for data, modules, artifacts, reports, collector output, and AI-ready evidence.",
      eyebrow = "Project",
      actions = ui_action_row(
        actionButton(ns("save_project"), "Save Project", class = "btn-primary"),
        actionButton(ns("load_project"), "Load Project", class = "btn-secondary")
      ),
      uiOutput(ns("workspace_overview")),
      uiOutput(ns("workspace_progress")),
      ui_workspace_grid(
        columns = "main-sidebar",
        tagList(
          ui_card(
            title = "Workspace Status",
            subtitle = "A compact readout of the current analytical run.",
            uiOutput(ns("project_message_panel")),
            uiOutput(ns("workspace_status")),

              "Recent Activity",

              level = "common",

              actionButton(ns("load_bundle"), "Load Bundle", class = "btn-secondary btn-sm")
            ),
            level = "advanced"
          )
        )
      )
    )
  )
}

## 408. 2026-07-08T15:37:20.476Z - current_projectless_megathread - assistant

Implemented the Artifact Studio demo seed and ran populated visual QA.

**What Changed**
- Added `create_artifact_studio_demo_project()` in [R/artifact_studio_demo_seed.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/artifact_studio_demo_seed.R).
- Added `qa_artifact_studio_demo_seed()`.
- Sourced the helper from [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/app.R).
- Fixed populated Studio rendering bugs in:
  - [R/page_artifact_library.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/page_artifact_library.R)
  - [R/ui_components.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/ui_components.R)

**Demo Output**
Generated under ignored `exports/`:

- Project RDS: `C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/exports/artifact_studio_demo/artifact_studio_demo_project.rds`
- Collector DOCX: `C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/exports/artifact_studio_demo/project_artifact_collector/Project_Artifact_Collector.docx`
- Collector manifest: `C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/exports/artifact_studio_demo/project_artifact_collector/Project_Artifact_Collector_manifest.csv`

The demo produces 30 real artifacts:
- 10 plots
- 17 tables
- 3 text/narrative artifacts
- 20 collector screenshots
- 68 table sidecars
- EDA, Model Readiness, SHAP Analysis, and collector append all ran.

**Bugs Found / Fixed**
- Populated gallery failed with `argument is of length zero`.
- Root causes:
  - zero-length artifact completeness values in quality handling
  - gallery lookup assumed list names always matched internal `artifact_id`
- Fixed with defensive quality scoring and artifact lookup by internal artifact id.

**Visual QA Punch List**
1. **Cards now populate correctly**: 30 gallery cards render, filmstrip shows real entries, inspector opens real table metadata/content.
2. **Filmstrip is useful**: populated state makes it feel like a real quick-switch lane.
3. **Cards still need real visual thumbnails**: plot cards currently show type/icon treatments, not the generated screenshot thumbnails. This is the biggest next polish opportunity.
4. **Inspector is useful but dense**: real table previews work, but the inspector will need hierarchy refinement once plot previews are embedded.
5. **Collector UI gap**: the demo creates real collector bundles/files, but loading a saved project still shows the live collector as `not_created` because current project loading recreates collector state later. I left this as a lifecycle gap ... [truncated]

- Use existing artifact/collector screenshot paths where available.
- Do not create a new screenshot pipeline.
- Plot cards should display real thumbnails.
- Table cards may show compact table preview/icon treatment.
- Narrative/diagnostic/recommendation cards may keep semantic icons.
- Inspector should show larger preview for plot artifacts.
- Missing thumbnails should gracefully fall back to current icon treatment.
- Preserve current empty state and layout.
- Do not implement Compare, Story Builder, Mission Control, or Agentic Lab.

- loading a saved seeded project should restore collector summary/status if collector files/manifest exist.
- do not redesign collector lifecycle.

- update qa_artifact_studio_demo_seed()
- verify plot thumbnails exist and render
- verify fallback works
- verify seeded project shows collector status after load
- existing QA still passes

- The user has the in-app browser open with 1 tab.
- Current URL: http://127.0.0.1:3899/

## My request for Codex:
# Task: Transform the Artifact Inspector into a Premium Evidence Inspector

## Background

Artifact Studio now successfully renders real artifacts.

The current implementation includes:

- Real plot thumbnails
- Artifact cards
- Persistent filmstrip
- Collector integration
- Real screenshot previews
- Quality metadata
- Diagnostics
- Recommendations
- Sidecar awareness

Technically, it works.

Experientially, it still feels like information is being displayed rather than investigated.

The Artifact Inspector should become one of the signature experiences of Analytics Workstation.

Think less "property panel."

Think more "Evidence Inspector."

---

# Objective

Redesign the Artifact Inspector around progressive disclosure and analytical storytelling.

The inspector should immediately communicate:

What am I looking at?

Why does it matter?

How good is it?

What should I do next?

Where did it come from?

Only after answering those questions should lower-level metadata appear.

---

# New Hierarchy

The inspector should visually prioritize information in roughly this order.

## Hero Preview

Large artifact preview.

For plots:

- large screenshot
- zoom-friendly sizing
- visually dominant

For tables:

- compact preview table
- or representative summary

For narratives:

- beautifully formatted narrative preview

---

## Executive Summary

Immediately below the preview.

Examples:

Caption

Purpose

Module

Run

Artifact Type

Analytical Intent

Importance

Render Targets

These should be readable at a glance.

---

## Quality Panel

Present quality as a first-class concept.

Include:

Quality Score

Completeness

Warnings

Collector Status

AI Readiness

Use the existing quality metadata.

Make this visually attractive.

---

## Diagnostics

Diagnostics should be expandable.

Do not overwhelm users immediately.

Include:

Warnings

Validation

Statistical Diagnostics

Risk Indicators

---

## Recommendations

Recommendations deserve their own section.

If available:

show them prominently.

If absent:

state that no recommendations were generated.

---

## Metadata

Move low-level metadata down.

Examples:

Artifact ID

Producer

Timestamp

Collector Path

Run ID

Render Targets

Policy Source

Quality Source

This becomes an advanced inspection area.

---

## Backing Assets

Provide a dedicated section.

Examples:

CSV

JSO ... [truncated]

## 435. 2026-07-08T17:00:40.930Z - current_projectless_megathread - assistant

Implemented the Artifact Inspector upgrade into a premium **Evidence Inspector**.

Changed:
- [R/page_artifact_library.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/page_artifact_library.R:492): rebuilt the right rail around Hero Preview, Executive Summary, Quality, Recommendations, expandable Diagnostics, advanced Metadata, and Backing Assets.
- [R/ui_components.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/ui_components.R:142): added reusable primitives: `ui_inspector_section()`, `ui_evidence_summary()`, `ui_quality_summary()`, `ui_metadata_grid()`, and `ui_backing_asset_panel()`.
- [www/app.css](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/www/app.css:1108): added dark workstation styling for the inspector dossier sections, quality panel, metadata grid, recommendations, diagnostics, and backing asset cards.
- [R/page_artifact_library.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/page_artifact_library.R:613): expanded `qa_artifact_studio()` to verify preview, summary, quality, diagnostics, recommendations, metadata, backing assets, progressive disclosure, and empty states.

Validation:
- `qa_artifact_studio()`: 21 success, 0 failures.
- `qa_artifact_studio_demo_seed()`: 15 success, 0 failures.
- `qa_analysis_modules_integration()`: Artifact Studio success, UI consistency success; one existing terminology historical-reference warning remains.
- `git diff --check`: clean, with only LF/CRLF warnings.
- Live browser visual QA: loaded seeded project with 30 artifacts, verified 10 real plot thumbnails, selected a plot artifact, confirmed hero screenshot preview, summary, quality, diagnostics, recommendations, backing assets, and no bogus screenshot-repair recommendation.

Remaining gap:
- In the live Project loader, a Windows backslash path was rejected once, while the same path with forward slashes loaded correctly. Artifact Studio itself is fine, but path normalization for project loading could use a small follow-up polish pass.


## 436. 2026-07-08T17:53:37.296Z - current_projectless_megathread - user

# Task: Normalize Project Load Paths

Fix project loading so Windows paths work reliably whether users paste:

- backslash paths
- forward slash paths
- quoted paths
- unquoted paths

Examples:
C:\Users\Bizon\Documents\...
C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/...
"C:\Users\Bizon\Documents\..."

Do not change Artifact Studio.

Add a small helper like normalize_project_load_path().

Use it wherever project RDS paths are accepted.

QA:
- backslash path loads
- forward slash path loads
- quoted path loads
- nonexistent path gives a clear error
- existing project loading still works

Run existing QA afterward.

page_project_ui <- function(id) {
  ns <- NS(id)

  tabPanel(
    "Project",
    ui_page(
      title = "Project Workspace",
      subtitle = "Mission control for data, modules, artifacts, reports, collector output, and AI-ready evidence.",
      eyebrow = "Project",
      actions = ui_action_row(
        actionButton(ns("save_project"), "Save Project", class = "btn-primary"),
        actionButton(ns("load_project"), "Load Project", class = "btn-secondary")
      ),
      uiOutput(ns("workspace_overview")),
      uiOutput(ns("workspace_progress")),
      ui_workspace_grid(
        columns = "main-sidebar",
        tagList(
          ui_card(
            title = "Workspace Status",
            subtitle = "A compact readout of the current analytical run.",
            uiOutput(ns("project_message_panel")),
            uiOutput(ns("workspace_status")),
            ui_disclosure(
              "Recent Activity",
              uiOutput(ns("recent_activity")),
              level = "common",
              open = TRUE
            )
          ),
          uiOutput(ns("ai_readiness_panel")),
          uiOutput(ns("collector_panel"))
        ),
        ui_card(
          title = "Project Files",
          subtitle = "Save or reload the project state and portable bundle.",
          textInput(
            ns("project_path"),
            "Project File",
            value = file.path(getwd(), "autoplots_project.rds")
          ),
          ui_action_row(
            actionButton(ns("save_project_secondary"), "Save", class = "btn-primary btn-sm"),
            actionButton(ns("load_project_secondary"), "Load", class = "btn-secondary btn-sm")
          ),
          ui_disclosure(
            "Bundle Options",
            textInput(
              ns("bundle_dir"),
              "Project Bundle Directory",
              value = file.path(getwd(), "autoplots_project")
            ),
            ui_action_row(
              actionButton(ns("save_bundle"), "Save Bundle", class = "btn-primary btn-sm"),
              actionButton(ns("load_bundle"), "Load Bundle", class = "btn-secondary btn-sm")
            ),
            level = "advanced"
          )
        )
      )
    )

## 439. 2026-07-08T18:00:35.840Z - current_projectless_megathread - user

# Task: Make Artifact Studio Feel Alive (Interaction & Flow Pass)

## Background

Artifact Studio is now functionally complete for Phase 1.

Implemented:

- Artifact Gallery
- Artifact Cards
- Real Plot Thumbnails
- Evidence Inspector
- Persistent Filmstrip
- Collector Integration
- Demo Seed
- Empty States
- QA

The next objective is NOT adding functionality.

The objective is making Artifact Studio feel alive.

Today it still behaves like a static page.

The goal is to create the feeling that users are exploring a living analytical workspace rather than browsing a collection of controls.

---

# Objective

Improve interaction quality.

Everything should feel responsive, intentional, and rewarding.

Do not add major new features.

Do not redesign Artifact Studio.

Do not modify backend architecture.

Focus on interaction quality.

---

# Interaction Principles

Every interaction should answer one question:

"What changed because I clicked?"

Avoid static transitions.

Provide visual feedback.

Create a sense of movement without unnecessary animation.

Motion should communicate state.

Never decorate for decoration's sake.

---

# Artifact Selection

When selecting an artifact:

Provide a clear transition.

Examples:

- selected card elevation
- glow/border transition
- inspector updates gracefully
- preview transition
- metadata transition

Avoid abrupt replacement.

---

# Inspector Transitions

When changing artifacts:

The inspector should feel like opening a new analytical dossier.

Examples:

- preview fades/slides
- summary updates smoothly
- quality panel updates
- diagnostics expand/collapse naturally

No loading flashes.

No layout jumps.

---

# Filmstrip

Improve interaction.

Examples:

hover preview

selection state

smooth scrolling

active artifact highlighting

maintain context

The filmstrip should feel like Lightroom's filmstrip rather than a static row of icons.

---

# Gallery

Improve card interaction.

Examples:

hover

selection

focus

status

quality indication

intent indication

collector status

Cards should invite exploration.

---

# Quality Feedback

Quality should feel alive.

Examples:

quality badge

collector status

artifact completeness

AI readiness

Warnings should naturally draw attention.

Success should feel satisfying.

---

# Progressive Discovery

The page should reward exploration.

Hovering should reveal useful information.

Clicking should reveal richer information.

Avoid overwhelming users immediately.

-- ... [truncated]

# Task: Build Mission Control (Phase 1)

## Background

Artifact Studio Phase 1 is now complete.

The application now has one genuine workstation mode centered around analytical evidence.

The next workstation mode is **Mission Control**.

Mission Control is **not** another dashboard.

It is the operational center of an analytics project.

Think:

NASA Mission Control

Bloomberg Terminal

Modern DevOps Operations Center

Air Traffic Control

Industrial Control Room

The user should immediately understand:

- What is happening?
- What is healthy?
- What needs attention?
- What should I do next?

Mission Control is about situational awareness.

---

# Philosophy

The Project is the world.

Artifacts are evidence.

Modules produce evidence.

The Collector preserves evidence.

Mission Control monitors the health of the entire analytical ecosystem.

It should feel like looking at the instrument panel of a sophisticated analytical machine.

---

# Objective

Build Mission Control Phase 1.

Do NOT redesign the application shell.

Do NOT redesign Artifact Studio.

Do NOT implement Agentic Lab.

Do NOT implement Workflow Graph.

Focus only on creating the operational command center.

---

# Initial Layout

Mission Control should contain approximately four major regions.

--------------------------------------------------------
Project Health
--------------------------------------------------------

Top-level health tiles.

Examples:

Project

Dataset

Current Run

Collector

AI Readiness

Artifact Quality

Workflow Status

Artifact Count

Warnings

Reports

--------------------------------------------------------
System Status
--------------------------------------------------------

Display current status of:

EDA

Model Readiness

Model Assessment

Model Insights

SHAP

Collector

Reports

QA

Each module should clearly communicate:

Not Started

Running

Completed

Warning

Failed

Future modules should automatically integrate.

---

--------------------------------------------------------
Alerts / Open Decisions
--------------------------------------------------------

This is NOT an error log.

It is the operational queue.

Examples:

Leakage suspected

Sparse segment

Missing target

Collector incomplete

Screenshot failures

Artifact quality warnings

Missing recommendations

Model drift

Render target incomplete

Future AI recommendations will naturally appear here.

---

----------------------------- ... [truncated]
